engine.name: GameEngine
engine.stageCompletion: 100% for the current core-first-mvp scope; not a commercial-engine completion claim.
stageClosureNotes: MVP closure is validated through the source-tree default build/test/agent contract plus documented optional desktop runtime and package lanes.
Apple/iOS/Metal validation remains host-gated and is not counted as complete without macOS/Xcode or CI evidence.
Generated-game, sample-game, desktop runtime, and desktop package readiness are limited to the validation recipes listed in this manifest.

languagePolicy.msvcCompileThroughput: MSVC targets use bounded compiler-level parallelism through MK_MSVC_MULTIPROCESSOR_COMPILE_PROCESSES default 2, emitting /MP2 plus /Zf from MK_apply_common_target_options while CMake/MSBuild owns target-level parallelism.
languagePolicy.msvcCompilePdbIsolation: MSVC compiler PDB files are target-named through MK_apply_common_target_options COMPILE_PDB_OUTPUT_DIRECTORY and COMPILE_PDB_NAME; the output directory stays short under the build tree so long linked-worktree paths do not trip C1041, and supported tools/*.ps1 CMake build entrypoints serialize overlapping CMake build invocations and clear stale Visual Studio .tlog directories with older aliased .lastbuildstate roots without adding /FS.
languagePolicy.msvcObjectPathHygiene: Long single-source test suites use compact CMake target names plus descriptive CTest names so Visual Studio $(IntDir) and .obj paths stay below MSVC path limits in linked worktrees.
languagePolicy.msvcIncrementalLinking: Disabled for linkable targets through MK_apply_common_target_options (/INCREMENTAL:NO) so validation builds do not depend on .ilk state.
languagePolicy.modules: Use CMake FILE_SET CXX_MODULES for project modules.
languagePolicy.importStd: Allowed only when CMake reports C++23 standard-library module support for the active generator/toolchain.

commands.removeMergedWorktree: pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/remove-merged-worktree.ps1 -WorktreePath <path> [-BaseRef origin/main] [-BaseBranch main] [-Remote origin] [-LocalCheckoutPath <path>] [-DeleteLocalBranch] [-DeleteRemoteBranch]
commands.postMergeTaskCleanup: pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/post-merge-task-cleanup.ps1 -WorktreePath <path> [-HeadRefOid <headRefOid>] [-BaseRef origin/main] [-BaseBranch main] [-Remote origin] [-LocalCheckoutPath <path>]

windowsDiagnosticsToolchain.policy: Official Microsoft host diagnostics only. These tools are not default build dependencies, are not installed by CMake configure, and are blockers only for tasks that explicitly need native debugging, D3D12 debug-layer validation, GPU capture, or ETW/performance evidence.

debuggingToolsForWindows.purpose: Native crash, dump, cdb, and windbg debugging for the Windows/MSVC lane.

graphicsTools.purpose: D3D12 debug layer support for Windows backend diagnostics.

pixOnWindows.purpose: D3D12 GPU captures, timing, and counter investigations.

windowsPerformanceToolkit.purpose: ETW tracing plus CPU and system performance investigations.

windowsDiagnosticsToolchain.hostGatePolicy: Missing Debugging Tools for Windows, Windows Graphics Tools, PIX on Windows, or Windows Performance Toolkit must be reported as a host diagnostics blocker only for the relevant diagnostic task; do not report it as an engine compile, CMake, or default validation failure.

modules.name: MK_audio
modules.purpose: Device-independent audio foundations. Currently provides an AudioMixer that validates audio buses, voices, spatial listener/source descriptors, clip metadata, device render requests, resampling quality, and interleaved float clip sample data; tracks logical clip asset ids; produces deterministic mix commands; builds spatial voice/listener metadata for distance attenuation and stereo pan; plans sample-frame voice scheduling; records streaming buffer availability, device format conversion intent, and underrun diagnostics; exposes AudioGameplayMixRequest, AudioGameplayMixPlan, and plan_gameplay_audio_mix so generated games can fail closed while converting game-owned cue, bus, fade, pause, loop, trigger, and optional spatial metadata rows into mixer-ready bus and voice commands; provides AudioStreamingQueue for decoded chunk append/consume/starvation state without codecs; renders deterministic interleaved float PCM buffers with gain, selectable nearest/linear resampling, channel conversion, and clamping; exposes AudioDeviceStreamRequest, AudioDeviceStreamPlan, plan_audio_device_stream, and render_audio_device_stream_interleaved_float so host adapters can fill output queues from AudioMixer PCM using bounded render chunks; adds AudioProductionReviewRequest, AudioProductionReadinessPlan, AudioProductionDecodedSourceEvidenceRow, AudioProductionStreamingChunkEvidenceRow, AudioProductionFormatConversionPolicyRow, AudioProductionDspGraphRow, AudioProductionDeviceLifecycleRow, AudioProductionUnsupportedClaimRow, and review_audio_production_readiness for side-effect-free reviewed decoded source, streaming chunk, conversion, bus/voice budget, DSP graph, spatial/listener, HRTF host gate, device lifecycle, unsupported claim, diagnostics, selected-package readiness, production-readiness, and replay-hash evidence; rejects broad codec support, audio middleware parity, native device handle exposure, background streaming execution, HRTF execution, device callback/IO, subjective mix-quality, incomplete rows, and budget overflow; and does not open OS audio devices.

modules.name: MK_audio_wasapi
modules.purpose: Windows WASAPI shared-mode audio adapter. Currently provides WasapiAudioRuntime for COM startup planning/lifetime, WasapiAudioDevice for default eRender/eConsole shared-mode playback through IAudioClient and IAudioRenderClient, queued-frame reporting through GetCurrentPadding, silence submission through AUDCLNT_BUFFERFLAGS_SILENT, interleaved float PCM queueing with float32 or pcm16 device-format handling, pause/resume/clear controls through Stop/Start/Reset, and side-effect-free planning rows for runtime startup, shared-mode stream setup, and endpoint queue writes. wasapi_audio_device_lifecycle_evidence records logical-device, audio-stream, queueing, pause/resume, clear, callback-free, host-evidence, and native-handle-free lifecycle rows for AudioProductionReviewRequest without exposing COM, endpoint, or WASAPI handles. It does not claim exclusive mode, capture, device-selection UI, spatial audio, hotplug parity, or broad latency readiness.

modules.name: MK_math
modules.purpose: Standard-library-only 2D/3D math primitives for games and engine systems, including vectors, matrices, unit quaternions, quaternion vector rotation, Mat4::rotation_quat, and transforms with position, scale, and radians-based rotation.

modules.name: MK_core
modules.purpose: Standard-library-only deterministic engine core. Currently provides GameApp and headless runner lifecycle contracts, logging contracts, fixed timestep behavior, entity ids, component registry, version data, dependency-free non-copyable/non-movable frame/worker scratch arena ownership through ScratchArena, ScratchLease, ScratchLeaseStatus, scratch_lease_status_label, reset_at_safe_point, padding-aware memory_counter_row evidence, and dependency-free diagnostics/counter/profile capture plus Trace Event JSON subset export through DiagnosticEvent, CounterSample, ProfileSample, DiagnosticCapture, DiagnosticSummary, DiagnosticsBudgetThresholds, DiagnosticsBudgetSummary, DiagnosticsBudgetStatus, ManualProfileClock, SteadyProfileClock, DiagnosticsRecorder, ScopedProfileZone, summarize_diagnostics, summarize_counter_budget, summarize_profile_budget, DiagnosticsTraceExportOptions, export_diagnostics_trace_json, DiagnosticsOpsHostStatus, DiagnosticsOpsPlanOptions, DiagnosticsOpsArtifact, DiagnosticsOpsPlan, build_diagnostics_ops_plan, MemoryLifetimeClass, MemoryCounterRow, MemoryClassDiagnosticsSummary, MemoryDiagnosticsSummary, MemoryBudgetPressure, MemoryDiagnosticsCode, MemoryDiagnosticsStatus, scratch reuse/reset/cross-thread-free/use-after-safe-point/false-sharing counters, summarize_memory_diagnostics, JobWorkerTopologyRow, JobQueueCounterRow, JobSchedulingWorkItemRow, JobSchedulingExecutionOptions, JobSchedulingExecutionOrderRow, JobSchedulingExecutionEvidence, JobSchedulingDiagnosticsSummary, JobSchedulingDiagnosticsCode, JobSchedulingDiagnosticsStatus, job_scheduling_diagnostics_code_label, job_scheduling_diagnostics_status_label, summarize_job_scheduling_diagnostics, and build_job_scheduling_execution_evidence for host-independent worker topology, deterministic bounded queue execution evidence, explicit dependency ids, worker-local output merge order, per-worker scratch MemoryCounterRow charges, and queue/steal/wait/merge/scratch/batch hazard evidence. JobExecutionPool, JobExecutionPoolDesc, JobExecutionBatchDesc, JobExecutionTaskDesc, JobExecutionContext, JobExecutionStopToken, JobExecutionRunResult, JobExecutionPoolStatus, JobExecutionRunStatus, JobExecutionDiagnosticCode, execute(batch), request_stop, stop_and_drain, worker_threads_started, and job_execution_* label helpers add a first-party persistent RAII std::thread worker pool with explicit worker_count, bounded worker queues, opt-in work_stealing_enabled execution, cooperative stop, batch drain semantics, worker-local ScratchArena ownership, deterministic publish evidence, runtime steal attempt/success/wait counters, value-only worker-start placement callback rows through JobExecutionWorkerPlacementRequest, JobExecutionWorkerPlacementResult, JobExecutionWorkerPlacementCallback, placement mode fields on JobExecutionPoolDesc, and worker placement attempt/applied/diagnostic/selected-CPU-Set run counters, plus fail-closed invalid-task/queue/dependency/exception/stopped diagnostics. JobExecutionTopologyPolicyDesc, JobExecutionTopologyPolicy, JobExecutionTopologyPolicyStatus, JobExecutionTopologyPolicyDiagnosticCode, select_job_execution_topology_policy, observe_job_execution_logical_processor_count, and job_execution_topology_policy_* label helpers add portable worker-count selection with fallback/cap/reserve rules, derived JobExecutionPoolDesc, optional work-stealing pool selection, processor-group evidence diagnostics, and NUMA evidence diagnostics. CpuSimdFeatureSet, CpuSimdLane, CpuSimdLaneRequest, SimdDispatchPolicyDesc, SimdDispatchPolicy, SimdDotProductEvidence, observe_cpu_simd_features, select_simd_dispatch_policy, compute_scalar_dot_product, build_simd_dot_product_evidence, and simd_dispatch_* label helpers add value-only scalar/SSE2/AVX2 CPU SIMD dispatch evidence over borrowed std::span<const float> inputs with AVX2 isolated in the target-local mirakana_core_avx2 OBJECT target and selected only when compile and runtime AVX register gates pass. MK_core still does not expose runtime, renderer, platform, editor, SDL3, OS, GPU, native thread handles, affinity masks, NUMA placement controls, ARM NEON, GPU compute dispatch, CUDA/HIP/SYCL paths, global allocator hooks/enforcement, broad pool policy, network upload, or Dear ImGui APIs.

modules.name: MK_ui
modules.purpose: First-party retained-mode runtime UI contracts for generated games and tools. Currently provides Runtime UI Workbench Production v1 through RuntimeUiWorkbenchDocument, RuntimeUiWorkbenchPanelRow, RuntimeUiWorkbenchTableRow, RuntimeUiWorkbenchGraphSeries, RuntimeUiWorkbenchItemRow, RuntimeUiWorkbenchTextInputFieldRow, RuntimeUiWorkbenchFocusPlan, RuntimeUiWorkbenchLocalizationRef, RuntimeUiWorkbenchAccessibilityRef, RuntimeUiWorkbenchPlan, and plan_runtime_ui_workbench for dense menu, inventory, equipment, shop, simulation dashboard, table, graph, focus, platform text-input request, localization, and accessibility intent rows with fail-closed backend/native/editor/RHI/UI-middleware term diagnostics and zero renderer/text-shaping/font-rasterization/IME/accessibility-bridge/image-decoding/native-platform adapter invocation. It also provides UiDocument retained element hierarchy with stable ids, parent/child relationships, visibility, enabled state, bounds, semantic roles, deterministic traversal, style/layout descriptors, headless row/column/stack layout solving with padding, margins, row/column gaps, anchors, fill, and size constraints, text metadata, image metadata, first-party renderer submission payload construction with solved element/layout data, RendererBox records, RendererTextRun records, image placeholder records, TextAdapterPayload rows with conservative line/span placeholders that keep resolved label text separate from localization keys and diagnose unresolved localization, an opt-in dependency-free MonospaceTextLayoutPolicy overload that emits deterministic logical-order line and glyph placeholder boxes with stable glyph ids, simple ASCII whitespace wrapping, clipping diagnostics, invalid-policy diagnostics, and unsupported right-to-left fallback diagnostics, ImageAdapterPayload rows with stable resource and asset URI references, AccessibilityPayload nodes carrying localization key, enabled, focusable, parent, and depth metadata, AccessibilityPublishPlan / AccessibilityPublishResult rows plus plan_accessibility_publish and publish_accessibility_payload validation before IAccessibilityAdapter dispatch, ImeCompositionPublishPlan / ImeCompositionPublishResult rows plus plan_ime_composition_update and publish_ime_composition validation before IImeAdapter dispatch, PlatformTextInputSessionPlan / PlatformTextInputSessionResult rows plus PlatformTextInputEndPlan / PlatformTextInputEndResult rows and plan_platform_text_input_session / begin_platform_text_input / plan_platform_text_input_end / end_platform_text_input validation before IPlatformIntegrationAdapter dispatch, TextEditState / CommittedTextInput / TextEditCommitPlan / TextEditCommitResult rows plus plan_committed_text_input and apply_committed_text_input validation/insertion/replacement, TextEditCommandKind / TextEditCommand / TextEditCommandPlan / TextEditCommandResult rows plus plan_text_edit_command and apply_text_edit_command validation for scalar-boundary cursor movement and backward/forward deletion, TextEditClipboardCommandKind / TextEditClipboardCommand / TextEditClipboardCommandPlan / TextEditClipboardCommandResult rows plus plan_text_edit_clipboard_command and apply_text_edit_clipboard_command validation for copy/cut/paste command application over TextEditState rows through IClipboardTextAdapter, TextShapingRequestPlan / TextShapingResult rows plus plan_text_shaping_request and shape_text_run validation before and after ITextShapingAdapter dispatch with strict-UTF-8 TextLayoutRun shaping rows, scalar-boundary TextShapingSegmentEvidence / TextBoundaryEvidence / cluster offsets, TextShapedGlyph, TextFontFallbackEvidence, resolved shaping direction/script/language, glyph ids/clusters/advances/offsets, fallback, and boundary evidence, a separate TextLineBreakRun contract for ILineBreakingAdapter rows that must not fabricate shaping glyph evidence, FontRasterizationRequestPlan / FontRasterizationResult rows plus plan_font_rasterization_request and rasterize_font_glyph validation before and after IFontRasterizerAdapter dispatch with GlyphRasterBitmap, GlyphRasterMetrics, FontRasterizationPixelFormat, glyph bitmap/metrics/pixel-format, zero-ink glyph evidence, and atlas allocation evidence, ImageDecodeRequestPlan / ImageDecodeDispatchResult rows plus plan_image_decode_request and decode_image_request validation before and after IImageDecodingAdapter dispatch, explicit adapter contracts and interfaces for text shaping, bidirectional text, production line breaking, font loading/rasterization, glyph atlas generation, native IME/text-input session integration, OS accessibility bridge publication, image decoding, concrete renderer adapters, and platform integration, input focus and navigation state with modal layer routing, UI transition state, one-way text binding, and command invocation without depending on editor, SDL3, Dear ImGui, UI middleware, native OS APIs, or GPU handles.

modules.name: MK_ui_renderer
modules.purpose: First-party runtime UI renderer adapter that consumes MK_ui RendererSubmission payloads through the existing MK_renderer public API without making MK_ui depend on renderer code. Currently provides UiRendererTheme token-to-color mapping, resolves styled UI boxes to renderer colors, converts RendererBox bounds into SpriteCommand rectangles, resolves deterministic MonospaceTextLayoutPolicy glyph rows through UiRendererGlyphAtlasPalette and UiRendererGlyphAtlasBinding by font_family plus glyph id into atlas-backed text glyph sprite submissions, resolves ImageAdapterPayload rows through UiRendererImagePalette and UiRendererImageBinding into image sprite submissions with deterministic resource_id-first and asset_uri-fallback lookup, can attach first-party cooked atlas page and UV metadata for glyph and image bindings, can build UiRendererImagePalette rows from MK_runtime RuntimeUiAtlasPayload package metadata for the native-ui-atlas-package-metadata proof, can build glyph palette rows from RuntimeUiAtlasGlyph package metadata through build_ui_renderer_glyph_atlas_palette_from_runtime_ui_atlas for the Runtime UI Glyph Atlas Package Bridge v1 proof, submits styled UI boxes, resolved text glyphs, and resolved image placeholders through IRenderer, reports text run, text adapter row, text glyph availability/resolution/missing/submission, image placeholder, image sprite submission, missing image resource, accessibility node, and adapter diagnostic availability, adds review_ui_renderer_atlas_handoff for side-effect-free cooked image/glyph atlas metadata, placement/budget/eviction, texture handoff, renderer counter, unsupported-claim, side-effect, and replay evidence review, and leaves concrete text shaping/font loading/rasterization/glyph atlas generation/source image decoding/production atlas packing/renderer texture upload/OS accessibility bridges behind their separate MK_ui adapter boundaries.

modules.name: MK_platform
modules.purpose: Platform-facing contracts. Currently provides VirtualInput for keyboard-style state, VirtualPointerInput for mouse/touch/pen-style pointer state, VirtualGamepadInput for gamepad buttons and axes, DisplayInfo and WindowDisplayState for monitor/DPI/safe-area metadata plus deterministic monitor selection and window placement policy without native handles, mobile lifecycle/orientation/safe-area/touch/storage/permission contracts for Android and Apple adapters, IClipboard and MemoryClipboard for text clipboard workflows, FileDialogRequest/IFileDialogService/MemoryFileDialogService for asynchronous native-style open/save/folder dialog workflows, CursorMode/ICursor/MemoryCursor for cursor visibility, confinement, and relative-input state, VirtualLifecycle for quit, termination, low-memory, background, and foreground lifecycle events, HeadlessWindow, MemoryFileSystem, RootedFileSystem, file watch backend selection with native backend family reporting, deterministic PollingFileWatcher file event scanning, WindowsFileWatcher backed by Win32 API ReadDirectoryChangesW event scanning behind PIMPL, LinuxFileWatcher backed by inotify event scanning, MacOSFileWatcher backed by FSEvents event scanning, host-unavailable native watcher diagnostics, shell-free ProcessCommand validation, a recording process runner, and a Windows CreateProcessW runner that captures stdout, stderr, exit code, and launch diagnostics without exposing native handles.

modules.name: MK_platform_win32
modules.purpose: Windows-native platform adapter foundation for the first-party desktop platform track. It registers private Win32 window classes, creates hidden or visible native windows through implementation-only Win32 APIs, pumps copied Win32 message rows into first-party Win32WindowEvent values, maps resize/move/focus/minimize/close/display/DPI rows into MK_platform window and lifecycle state, exposes only first-party values plus opaque integer tokens for diagnostics, plans cursor modes, translates copied keyboard/mouse/modifier rows into VirtualInput and VirtualPointerInput, records keyboard/mouse-scoped Raw Input registration plans, provides CF_UNICODETEXT clipboard read/write adapters, plans and launches Common Item Dialog open/save/folder requests through FileDialogService rows, maps minimal UTF-16 committed text plus text-edit and clipboard shortcut rows into MK_ui contracts, and exposes host-owned Windows CPU Sets worker-placement rows through Win32CpuSetRow, Win32CpuSetWorkerPlacementPlan, query_win32_cpu_sets, select_win32_cpu_set_worker_placement, and make_win32_cpu_set_worker_placement_callback over the MK_core worker-start placement callback without exposing HWND, HINSTANCE, HCURSOR, MSG, WPARAM, LPARAM, COM, Raw Input, Common Item Dialog, HANDLE, affinity masks, CPU Set native handles, or SDL3 types in public headers. Win32 CPU Sets placement also records SMT-focused value counters for distinct CoreIndex count, SMT sibling CPU Set count, SMT topology known, SMT policy applied, and worker-row SMT sibling deferral when avoid_smt_siblings is selected. Controller execution, full TSF composition/candidate UI, cross-platform runtime host wiring, editor shell migration, Linux affinity, NUMA allocation execution, hybrid P-core/E-core pinning, broad SMT scheduling quality, broad SIMD quality, GPU async overlap, CUDA/HIP/SYCL, and broader audio policy remain later phases; SDL3 adapters have been removed and must not re-enter public platform headers.

MAVG RHI Indirect Draw v1 adds indirect_draw.hpp, IndexedIndirectDrawCommand, IndexedIndirectDrawDesc, BufferUsage::indirect, IRhiCommandList::draw_indexed_indirect, indirect-draw RhiStats rows, Null RHI argument/count-buffer deterministic execution counters, and Vulkan VK_BUFFER_USAGE_INDIRECT_BUFFER_BIT mapping while D3D12 ExecuteIndirect, Vulkan indirect draw execution, Metal indirect command buffers, native handles, Nanite equivalence/superiority, and broad optimization remain unclaimed.

MAVG Conventional Indexed Draw Range v1 changes rhi::IRhiCommandList::draw_indexed to the clean-break five-argument contract (index_count, instance_count, first_index, vertex_offset, first_instance), adds RhiStats last indexed range rows, and forwards the range to Null RHI, D3D12 DrawIndexedInstanced, and Vulkan vkCmdDrawIndexed. Focused evidence covers MK_rhi_tests, MK_d3d12_rhi_tests, MK_backend_scaffold_tests, and MK_renderer_tests without indirect draws, command signatures, mesh shader dispatch, package streaming execution, backend handle exposure, or broad optimization claims.

RHI Native Async Upload Execution v1 adds execute_upload_gpu_batch_async plus RhiUploadGpuBatchExecutionResult so caller-owned RhiUploadStagingPlan/RhiUploadRing batches can prevalidate target counts and ring ownership, submit pending copies on a selected backend-neutral QueueKind, mark staging allocations with the returned FenceValue, and avoid helper-owned CPU waits without exposing native handles or claiming async overlap/performance.

Staging Pool Lease Adoption v1 adds RhiStagingBufferLease, RhiStagingBufferPool::try_acquire_lease, RhiStagingBufferPool::release(RhiStagingBufferLease), and RhiUploadRingDesc::buffer so explicit host-owned pool chunks can back caller-owned RhiUploadRing uploads without extra ring buffer allocation.
modules.purpose: Graphics-API-independent RHI contracts for backend capability/probe profiles, host support policy, shader artifact formats, backend bootstrap steps, buffers, textures including shared texture usage intent, samplers, swapchains, explicit swapchain frame acquire/release, backend-neutral swapchain resize, shaders, descriptor set layouts, descriptor sets, upload-buffer writes, copy/upload/readback commands with explicit texture footprints, CPU buffer readback, transient resource leases, presentation ownership and sequencing, single-pending-frame swapchain throttling, graphics pipelines with explicit vertex input layouts/attributes plus DepthStencilStateDesc depth-test/write/compare state, render passes with explicit clear colors plus optional RenderPassDepthAttachment and ClearDepthValue, descriptor set binding including sampler descriptors, vertex/index buffer binding, direct/indexed draw calls including range-aware indexed draws, resource transitions, texture aliasing barrier intent through IRhiCommandList::texture_aliasing_barrier and RhiStats::texture_aliasing_barriers for distinct texture handles plus public TextureHandle{} wildcard/null endpoints, including D3D12 backend-private null-resource aliasing barrier evidence tracked by DeviceContextStats::null_resource_aliasing_barriers, D3D12 transient texture lease counters through RhiStats::transient_texture_heap_allocations/RhiStats::transient_texture_placed_allocations/RhiStats::transient_texture_placed_resources_alive and DeviceContextStats::placed_resource_activation_barriers plus Vulkan transient alias-group allocation evidence through the same RhiStats counters, fences, backend-neutral write/copy/read/fence/sampler lifecycle stats, shared texture export/failure stats, and a NullRhiDevice for backend-free validation of depth attachment ownership, usage, format, clear-depth range, extent, and pipeline color/depth compatibility plus null_mark_buffer_released/null_mark_texture_released/null_mark_sampler_released/null_mark_descriptor_set_released/null_mark_descriptor_set_layout_released/null_mark_pipeline_layout_released helpers for deterministic safe-point invalidation during host-owned teardown validation. Renderer/RHI Resource Foundation v1 adds a foundation-only value API in resource_lifetime.hpp through RhiResourceLifetimeRegistry, generation-checked first-party resource handles, explicit resource kinds, owner labels, debug names, live/deferred-release records, queue-qualified fence retirement, deterministic diagnostics, and enum-backed marker-style lifetime event rows. Upload/Staging Foundation v1 adds RhiUploadStagingPlan in upload_staging.hpp with staging allocation ids, buffer and texture copy records, deterministic copy-range diagnostics, submitted FenceValue tracking, and completed-fence retirement. Upload ring and staging pool execution v1 adds RhiUploadRing fence-tracked suballocations over one copy_source upload buffer, RhiStagingBufferPool freelists, RhiStagingBufferLease, RhiStagingBufferPool::try_acquire_lease, RhiStagingBufferPool::release(RhiStagingBufferLease), RhiUploadRingDesc::buffer, validate_upload_gpu_batch, record_upload_gpu_batch, and mark_pending_allocations_submitted for IRhiCommandList buffer and buffer-to-texture copies with null-backend readback proof. These foundations do not migrate native backend destruction, implement vendor-specialized async GPU upload queues or streaming package integration, a GPU allocator, residency budgets, package streaming, GPU markers beyond the scoped API, editor resource management/capture tooling execution beyond reviewed request handoff, production renderer scheduling, or 2D/3D playable vertical-slice readiness. The D3D12 IRhiDevice bridge implements private DSV-backed public depth attachments, native depth pipeline state, active pass/pipeline color-depth format validation, readback-observable depth-test proof, sampled-depth proof, depth-aware postprocess proof, selected height-fog constants/depth postprocess proof, and directional shadow receiver proof; the Vulkan bridge implements private depth-aspect image views for sampled descriptors, depth|stencil D24S8 barrier aspect masks, required synchronization2 feature query/device enablement, depth_write synchronization2 barriers, dynamic-rendering depth attachments, native depth pipeline state, active pass/pipeline color-depth format validation, environment-gated depth-ordered readback proof, environment-gated visible sampled-depth readback proof, environment-gated postprocess-depth readback proof, environment-gated height-fog runtime readback proof through explicit MK_VULKAN_TEST_HEIGHT_FOG_* artifacts plus VK_LAYER_KHRONOS_validation readiness, environment-gated physical-sky runtime readback proof through explicit MK_VULKAN_TEST_PHYSICAL_SKY_VERTEX_SPV and MK_VULKAN_TEST_PHYSICAL_SKY_FRAGMENT_SPV artifacts plus VK_LAYER_KHRONOS_validation readiness, and environment-gated directional shadow receiver readback proof while keeping Vulkan handles private.

applications.name: MK_editor
applications.purpose: Optional desktop editor shell built by the dependency-free `desktop-editor` lane. The previous SDL3/Dear ImGui shell has been replaced in the active `MK_editor` target by a clean Win32 first-party retained shell owned privately under `editor/src`, with `MK_editor_core` remaining the supported editor logic and AI-operable retained-model surface for project IO, authoring, core-backed `EditorDockLayout` / `editor_dock_panel_catalog` dock and panel identity rows, value-only `EditorDockCommandKind` / `EditorDockCommandRequest` / `EditorDockCommandPlan` dock mutation planners, core-backed `EditorRichText*` document/selection/copy/view-model/AI rows, profiler rows, generated game studio rows, input rebinding rows, play-in-editor review rows, game-module driver tests, material preview retained rows, and the first native panel set. The shell must not depend on SDL3, Dear ImGui, Qt, Slint, RmlUi, or UI middleware, and must not expose Win32, D3D12, DXGI, DirectWrite, TSF, UIA, COM, swapchain, descriptor heap, clipboard, process, file-dialog, texture, or UI-middleware handles through public editor, engine, gameplay, or runtime UI APIs.

recommendedNextPlan.latestCloseoutEvidence: MAVG Vulkan GPU Culling Dispatch v1 (`mavg-vulkan-gpu-culling-dispatch-v1`) completed through PR #563 (merge commit `5dce6857`). The completed slice adds `dispatch_mavg_gpu_culling_indirect` in `engine/rhi/vulkan/src/vulkan_mavg_gpu_culling_dispatch.cpp`: the first Vulkan compute dispatch writes MAVG packed indexed indirect argument and count buffers from reviewed cluster rows with compute-write-to-indirect-read synchronization through `VK_KHR_synchronization2` buffer_memory_barrier2 rows, sets `executed_gpu_culling=true` only after successful dispatch, and proves visible and culled cluster cases through SPIR-V environment-gated `MK_mavg_vulkan_gpu_culling_dispatch_tests` while `MK_mavg_gpu_culling_tests` retain `executed_gpu_culling=false` value-only planner evidence on top of completed `mavg_gpu_culling.hpp` / `MavgGpuCullingIndirectPlan` / `plan_mavg_gpu_culling_indirect_commands` / `MavgGpuCullingSyncRequirement` rows. MAVG GPU Culling Dispatch v1 (`mavg-gpu-culling-dispatch-v1`) completed through PR #556. The completed slice adds `dispatch_mavg_gpu_culling_indirect` in `engine/rhi/d3d12/src/d3d12_mavg_gpu_culling_dispatch.cpp`: the first D3D12 compute dispatch writes MAVG packed indexed indirect argument and count buffers from reviewed cluster rows with compute-write-to-indirect-read synchronization through D3D12 UAV barriers and `D3D12_RESOURCE_STATE_INDIRECT_ARGUMENT` transitions, sets `executed_gpu_culling=true` only after successful dispatch, and proves visible and culled cluster cases through WARP-backed `MK_mavg_gpu_culling_dispatch_tests` while `MK_mavg_gpu_culling_tests` retain `executed_gpu_culling=false` value-only planner evidence on top of completed `mavg_gpu_culling.hpp` / `MavgGpuCullingIndirectPlan` / `plan_mavg_gpu_culling_indirect_commands` / `MavgGpuCullingSyncRequirement` rows. MAVG Vulkan Count-Buffer Indirect Execution v1 (`mavg-vulkan-count-buffer-indirect-execution-v1`) completed through PR #552. The completed slice adds count-buffer Vulkan execution where `IRhiCommandList::draw_indexed_indirect` runs `vkCmdDrawIndexedIndirectCount` with 4-byte-aligned count-buffer offsets, executing `min(count_buffer_value, max_draw_count)` draws for CPU-generated upload argument and count buffers, reuses shared `decode_indexed_indirect_count_buffer_value` / `effective_indexed_indirect_draw_count` helpers, increments `indexed_indirect_count_buffer_reads`, sets `last_indexed_indirect_count_buffer_value` from the decoded upload count and `last_indexed_indirect_executed_draw_count` to the effective executed command count, and proves count-limited, zero-count, and invalid-input coverage through SPIR-V environment-gated `MK_backend_scaffold_tests` on top of completed `indirect_draw.hpp` / `IndexedIndirectDrawCommand` / `IndexedIndirectDrawDesc` / `BufferUsage::indirect` / `IRhiCommandList::draw_indexed_indirect` contract evidence. MAVG D3D12 Count-Buffer Indirect Execution v1 (`mavg-d3d12-count-buffer-indirect-execution-v1`) completed through PR #547. The completed slice adds D3D12 count-buffer indirect execution where `IRhiCommandList::draw_indexed_indirect` runs `ID3D12GraphicsCommandList::ExecuteIndirect` with a non-null count resource and 4-byte-aligned `CountBufferOffset`, executing `min(count_buffer_value, max_draw_count)` draws for CPU-generated upload argument and count buffers, adds shared `decode_indexed_indirect_count_buffer_value` / `effective_indexed_indirect_draw_count` helpers plus a bounded `decode_indexed_indirect_draw_commands` overload, increments `indexed_indirect_count_buffer_reads`, sets `last_indexed_indirect_count_buffer_value` from the decoded upload count and `last_indexed_indirect_executed_draw_count` to the effective executed command count, and proves count-limited, zero-count, and invalid-input coverage through WARP-backed `MK_d3d12_rhi_tests` on top of completed `indirect_draw.hpp` contract evidence. MAVG Vulkan Indexed Indirect Draw Execution v1 (`mavg-vulkan-indexed-indirect-draw-execution-v1`) completed through PR #541 with `vkCmdDrawIndexedIndirect` execution for CPU-generated upload indexed indirect argument buffers without count buffers. MAVG D3D12 Indexed Indirect Draw Execution v1 (`mavg-d3d12-indexed-indirect-draw-execution-v1`) completed through PR #537 conventional upload re-land, PR #538 GPU culling plus RHI indirect contract re-land, and PR #539 D3D12 ExecuteIndirect execution re-land with backend-private `D3D12_INDIRECT_ARGUMENT_TYPE_DRAW_INDEXED` command signatures and visible WARP-backed texture readback proof in `MK_d3d12_rhi_tests`. MAVG Vulkan Compute-Generated Indirect Consumption v1 (`mavg-vulkan-compute-generated-indirect-consumption-v1`) completed through PR #567 / merge commit `9c6b681f`: Vulkan `draw_indexed_indirect` consumes GPU-written MAVG packed argument/count buffers through `is_compute_generated_indexed_indirect_buffer`, `external_argument_buffer`, `external_count_buffer`, `leave_indirect_argument_state_for_consumption`, backend-private synchronization2 barriers, and `MK_mavg_vulkan_compute_generated_indirect_consumption_tests`. MAVG D3D12 Compute-Generated Indirect Consumption v1 (`mavg-d3d12-compute-generated-indirect-consumption-v1`) completed through PR #560. MAVG Payload Byte-Range Page Loader v1 (`mavg-payload-byte-range-page-loader-v1`) adds mavg_payload_page_loader.hpp, RuntimeMavgPayloadPageLoadDesc, RuntimeMavgPayloadPageLoadResult, and load_runtime_mavg_payload_pages for side-effect-free caller-owned .mavgpayload byte-range extraction. Mesh shaders, Metal readiness, Nanite equivalence/superiority, filesystem byte-range IO, background streaming, GPU memory pressure integration, and broad optimization remain unclaimed. Renderer Backend Parity Host Recipe Proof v1 remains completed evidence, SDL3 stays absent, and unsupportedProductionGaps = [].

perf stat gathers performance counter statistics for a command or target.
perf record captures sampled profiles into perf.data for later reporting.
VTune CLI supports scripted collection, reporting, import, and before/after comparison workflows.
VTune Hotspots identifies CPU time hotspots and reports utilization views.
AMD uProf Hotspots and CLI workflows are host/tool-version dependent and can collect CPU time and hardware counter evidence.

cpuProfilingMatrix.notes: The matrix is schema/static-check visible, but every host row is host-gated until official profiler artifacts are attached. Missing host tools or missing counter support must be reported as host-gated evidence, not ready, blocked, or broad optimization.

optionalGpuComputeReview.summary: Optional GPU Compute Review v1 classifies CUDA, HIP, SYCL, RHI compute, and offline acceleration candidates before any vendor runtime, toolchain dependency, backend path, or default validation requirement is introduced.

requiredEvidence: explicit barriers, semaphores, fences, or queue waits
candidateRows.notes: Runtime rendering, skinning, morph, simulation, and package-residency workloads prefer backend-neutral RHI compute instead of CUDA/HIP/SYCL so D3D12/Vulkan/Metal ownership and gameplay APIs remain first-party.
Offline cook, import, compression, analysis, or bake tools may consider GPU acceleration only when deterministic output, legal/dependency records, and scalar fallback evidence are attached.

requiredEvidence: device properties including asyncEngineCount when relevant
candidateRows.notes: CUDA/HIP may be reviewed only as optional tooling or private adapter research. It must not add default runtime linkage, public handles, default validation dependencies, or broad cross-vendor claims.

requiredEvidence: queue profiling enabled when timing is claimed
candidateRows.notes: SYCL may be reviewed only as optional tooling or private adapter research with explicit implementation/backend install gates. It is not a default runtime or validation dependency.
A default engine runtime dependency on CUDA, HIP, or SYCL is rejected for this milestone because it would make default validation host/vendor fragile and duplicate RHI backend ownership.

fallbackRequirements: offline tool candidates require scalar fallback
private adapter candidates require install-gated opt-in feature selection
all candidates must fail closed when device, backend, profiler, or synchronization proof is unavailable

officialReferences.notes: CUDA async overlap and deployment depend on measured transfers, device capability, streams, and runtime/toolkit availability.
HIP asynchronous work uses streams and explicit synchronization; overlap depends on device/runtime support.
SYCL device/backend availability, queue events, USM/buffer choices, and profiling properties are implementation dependent.
Vulkan queue ordering and cross-queue execution require explicit synchronization.
D3D12 multi-engine work requires explicit fence timelines and queue synchronization.

optionalGpuComputeReview.notes: This review is machine-visible evidence selection only. It does not implement CUDA, HIP, SYCL, RHI compute changes, GPU async overlap, new package counters, CMake/vcpkg features, default validation requirements, or backend parity claims.

packageStreamingResidencyLoops.summary: Descriptor package streaming and residency budget contract for explicitly selected cooked runtime scene packages. It lets agents select safe manifest package candidates, require runtime scene validation preflight, and record resident budget intent while broad background streaming, arbitrary eviction, public native/RHI handles, Metal readiness, and renderer quality claims remain unsupported.

orderedSteps.notes: Select a descriptor row that references a manifest runtimeSceneValidationTargets id and a manifest runtimePackageFiles .geindex entry.
Run the existing non-mutating package load plus runtime scene instantiation preflight before treating budget intent as usable.
Review positive byte budgets and optional resource-kind or asset-key hints as intent only; no allocator or budget enforcement is implied.
Use the existing host-owned renderer resource upload loop when GPU upload evidence is needed. Gameplay still does not receive IRhiDevice, native handles, or SceneGpuBindingPalette.
Selected safe-point replacement execution is available only through the host-gated package streaming execution loop; broad async/background streaming and arbitrary eviction stay unsupported.

packageStreamingResidencyLoops.notes: This loop makes package streaming and residency-budget intent machine-readable for selected host-gated safe-point execution. It relies on existing runtime scene validation and host-owned resource upload gates, does not add command strings, and does not make broad async/background streaming, arbitrary eviction, allocator/GPU budget enforcement, Metal readiness, or renderer quality ready.

packageStreamingResidencyLoops.summary: Selected host-gated package streaming execution for one manifest packageStreamingResidencyTargets row. It requires validate-runtime-scene-package preflight evidence, consumes a loaded RuntimeAssetPackageLoadResult for package mount/replace paths, validates required preload asset hints, resident resource kind hints, and selected resident package count hints, stages, mounts, replaces, or unmounts explicit resident packages, commits package/catalog or resident mount/cache changes together at a safe point, and reports deterministic diagnostics without broad background streaming, arbitrary eviction, allocator/GPU budget enforcement, renderer/RHI teardown, public native/RHI handles, Metal readiness, or renderer quality claims.

orderedSteps.notes: Select exactly one host-gated-safe-point descriptor row that references a manifest runtimeSceneValidationTargets id and a manifest runtimePackageFiles .geindex entry.
The helper requires successful runtime scene validation evidence before staging a loaded replacement package.
Package file loading stays separate from safe-point commit; load failures produce diagnostics and leave active package/catalog unchanged.
Stages the loaded package only after preflight succeeds and commits through commit_runtime_package_safe_point_replacement for the single-store package/catalog path.
Mounts an already-loaded package under a new explicit RuntimeResidentPackageMountIdV2 and refreshes RuntimeResidentCatalogCacheV2 only after descriptor, hint, budget, and raw catalog preflights pass.

Replaces one existing RuntimeResidentPackageMountIdV2 through commit_runtime_resident_package_replace_v2, reports resident_replace rows, and preserves the live mount set plus cached catalog on invalid id, missing id, candidate catalog, budget, or cache-refresh failure.

Removes one existing RuntimeResidentPackageMountIdV2 through commit_runtime_resident_package_unmount_v2, reports resident_unmount rows, validates projected remaining preload/kind/count hints before live mutation, and preserves the live mount set plus cached catalog on invalid id, missing id, residency hint, budget, or cache-refresh failure.
Reports status, selected target ids, estimated resident bytes, resident budget intent, replacement status, stale-handle counts, and diagnostics.
Renderer/RHI upload and deferred teardown stay in host-owned renderer/RHI loops; gameplay and manifests still do not receive IRhiDevice, native handles, descriptor handles, or GPU residency objects.

packageStreamingResidencyLoops.notes: Host-gated selected package streaming execution uses first-party runtime package/catalog handles and explicit resident mount ids only. Resident budget is an intent check with diagnostics, not allocator or GPU memory budget enforcement. Resident replacement uses execute_selected_runtime_package_streaming_resident_replace_safe_point over an already-loaded package; resident unmount uses execute_selected_runtime_package_streaming_resident_unmount_safe_point over an existing mount id and projected remaining catalog hints. Broad disk/VFS package mount orchestration beyond reviewed eviction-assisted candidate resident mount, reviewed eviction-assisted candidate resident replacement, discovery-selected resident commit, and candidate resident mount/replacement, background streaming, arbitrary eviction, renderer/RHI ownership, upload/staging package integration, and broad hot reload productization remain unsupported.

safePointPackageReplacementLoops.summary: Host-owned safe-point package replacement and unload execution for preloaded runtime packages. Replacement rebuilds a RuntimeResourceCatalogV2 from the pending package, commits the RuntimeAssetPackageStore and catalog together only at a safe point, and rolls back invalid pending packages before active swap. Unload clears the active package and commits an empty catalog only at a safe point, discarding pending replacement state when an active package exists. Both paths report deterministic generation and stale-handle invalidation counters without executing async streaming, eviction, renderer/RHI teardown, public native/RHI handles, Metal readiness, or renderer quality claims.

orderedSteps.notes: Only a successfully loaded RuntimeAssetPackage may enter pending state; failed package loads leave the active package untouched.
Build a candidate catalog from the pending package before touching the active package or active catalog.
Swap the pending package into active state and replace the catalog only after the candidate catalog succeeds.
Duplicate or invalid package records keep the existing active package/catalog live and clear the pending package.

Clear the active package and replace the catalog with an empty generation only when an active package exists; pending replacements are discarded on successful unload and preserved when no active package exists.
Renderer/RHI teardown and deferred release remain host-owned follow-up work; gameplay and manifests still do not receive IRhiDevice, native handles, descriptor handles, or GPU residency objects.

safePointPackageReplacementLoops.notes: This is a C++ runtime helper for host-owned safe points after an explicit package has already been loaded and validated. It does not load package files, parse manifests, run shell commands, cook assets, stream packages in the background, execute renderer/RHI teardown, or expose native handles.

The selected smoke builds UiRendererGlyphAtlasPalette rows from RuntimeUiAtlasGlyph metadata, submits one deterministic engine-default glyph through MK_ui_renderer, and reports text_glyphs_available, text_glyphs_resolved, text_glyphs_missing, text_glyph_sprites_submitted, ui_atlas_metadata_glyphs, and texture overlay bind/draw counters without runtime source font loading or glyph atlas generation.

rendererBackendAssumptions.vulkan: not selected for this generated 3D glyph atlas smoke until a separate Vulkan package recipe lands

unsupportedClaims: public native or RHI handle access

requestShape.pathPolicy: gameManifest must be games/<game_name>/game.agent.json and patch paths must stay within known manifest fields.

capabilityGates.notes: The patch must stay inside the manifest contract validated by schema-check.
Agent-facing manifest changes must keep agent-check green.

undoToken.notes: Undo tokens are placeholders until broad manifest patch apply is implemented.

commandSurfaces.notes: Descriptor-only; use the existing narrow register-runtime-package-files command for ready package-file appends.

commandSurfaces.summary: Dry-run or execute a reviewed allowlist of manifest-declared validation recipes with host-gate diagnostics and no arbitrary shell input.

requestModes.notes: Resolves a reviewed argv command plan through tools/run-validation-recipe.ps1 without launching commands.
Executes only the reviewed command plan built by Get-ValidationRecipeCommandPlan and reports exit code, duration, stdout summary, and stderr summary.

requestShape.pathPolicy: Recipe ids must come from the reviewed run-validation-recipe allowlist. tools/run-validation-recipe.ps1 does not evaluate raw manifest command strings, is not arbitrary shell, rejects free-form arguments, and builds argv only through reviewed command builders.

capabilityGates.notes: Only manifest-declared validation recipes may be selected.

undoToken.notes: Validation command execution is non-mutating, but structured command results still carry the placeholder undo field.
