Invoke-ValidationRecipeCommandPlan executes reviewed argv entries with ProcessStartInfo.ArgumentList; package-script recipes with hyphen-prefixed SmokeArgs use fixed reviewed PowerShell -Command wrappers so the array reaches tools/package-desktop-runtime.ps1 without evaluating raw manifest command strings. The runner is not arbitrary shell, and free-form arguments are rejected. It does not provide a CI service, package mutation, editor productization, shader graph/live shader generation, package streaming, native handle exposure, Metal precipitation execution outside the selected Apple-host recipe, Vulkan precipitation by inference beyond the strict selected rain lane, broad Metal readiness outside the selected Apple-host recipe, Vulkan readiness by inference, Vulkan volumetric-fog readiness beyond the selected strict lane, Metal volumetric-fog readiness outside the selected Apple-host recipe, Vulkan IBL readiness beyond the selected strict lane, broad environment readiness, broad CPU/GPU/memory optimization, broad long-running readiness, or general production renderer quality. Selected environment package smokes also emit fail-closed environment_quality_budget_* counters and keep environment_quality_budget_broad_environment_ready_claimed=0.

authoringSurfaces.notes: Headless, DesktopRuntimePackage, DesktopRuntimeCookedScenePackage, DesktopRuntimeMaterialShaderPackage, DesktopRuntime2DPackage, and DesktopRuntime3DPackage scaffolds are validated by agent/static checks and focused desktop runtime/package recipes.

Game manifests expose backend readiness, importer requirements, packaging targets, runtimePackageFiles, runtimeSceneValidationTargets, atlasTilemapAuthoringTargets, prefabScenePackageAuthoringTargets, registeredSourceAssetCookTargets, packageStreamingResidencyTargets, and validation recipes for generated games. runtimeSceneValidationTargets rows are descriptor-only inputs for validate-runtime-scene-package; atlasTilemapAuthoringTargets rows are deterministic data-only 2D tilemap package authoring descriptors; prefabScenePackageAuthoringTargets rows are deterministic 3D Scene/Prefab v2 plus selected SourceAssetRegistry package authoring descriptors; registeredSourceAssetCookTargets rows are descriptor-only cook-registered-source-assets inputs paired to prefab authoring rows with explicit or registry-closure dependency expansion; packageStreamingResidencyTargets rows are selected host-gated safe-point package streaming descriptors and do not add shell commands, runtime source parsing, renderer/RHI residency execution, broad background streaming, arbitrary eviction, editor productization, or native handles.
GUI-independent v0 authoring models exist, but they are not yet the future stable-id Scene/Component/Prefab Schema v2 command layer.

Contract-only stable-id scene/component/prefab v2 value APIs, validation, deterministic text IO, stable prefab override path composition, deterministic prefab source provenance rows through SceneNodePrefabSourceV2 / SceneComponentPrefabSourceV2 `prefab_source` text fields, non-mutating stable-id prefab instance refresh planning through ScenePrefabInstanceRefreshPlanV2, ScenePrefabInstanceRefreshRowV2, and plan_scene_prefab_instance_refresh_v2 comparing a selected instance root against PrefabDocumentV2 source_node_id / source_component_id rows, fail-closed duplicate_prefab_source_identity diagnostics for duplicate copied source identities, unsupported_nested_prefab_instance diagnostics for descendant nested prefab roots, unsupported_local_prefab_child / unsupported_local_prefab_component diagnostics for local selected-subtree rows without selected-prefab provenance, and pure value stable-id prefab instance refresh apply through ScenePrefabInstanceRefreshResultV2 and apply_scene_prefab_instance_refresh_v2 are implemented in MK_scene. The value apply preserves matched node/component ids and state, adds deterministic instance_root/refresh/<source-id> rows, removes stale sourced rows, returns source-to-result mappings, and does not create editor actions, implement runtime prefab semantics, or perform nested propagation. Reviewed MK_tools dry-run/apply authoring commands now operate on this contract, instantiate-prefab records source prefab path plus source node/component ids, refresh-prefab-instance wraps the stable-id value apply into a reviewed safe .scene write surface, and the separate migrate-scene-v2-runtime-package surface bridges supported Scene v2 rows into the existing Scene v1 package update helper. A focused command-authored workflow proof now drives apply_source_asset_registration -> apply_registered_source_asset_cook_package -> apply_scene_v2_runtime_package_migration -> execute_runtime_scene_package_validation, including perspective camera and directional light cone-field projection. Source asset registration is ready through GameEngine.SourceAssetRegistry.v1. Editor migration, nested prefab propagation/merge resolution UX, broad/dependent package cooking, and 2D/3D vertical slices remain follow-up work.

Runtime Scene Gameplay Binding v1 is a backend-neutral value contract for generated gameplay authoring: RuntimeSceneGameplayBindingSourceRow rows carry binding_id, gameplay_system_id, slot_id, node_name, and a required RuntimeSceneGameplayBindingComponentKind, and resolve_runtime_scene_gameplay_bindings maps them to RuntimeSceneGameplayBindingRow node ids only when authored scene names are unique and required camera, light, mesh_renderer, sprite_renderer, or any_renderable component gates pass. Diagnostics cover invalid ids, duplicate binding ids, missing or duplicate node names, and missing required components; failed resolutions clear partial bindings. Selected 2D/3D package smokes expose gameplay_systems_scene_binding_ready and exact source/binding/system/component row counters before generated-game quality gates treat scene wiring as evidenced. This is not a gameplay system scheduler, script runtime, editor command surface, renderer/RHI execution path, package format change, or broad generated-game production readiness claim.

Runtime Scene Gameplay Interaction Framework v1 is a backend-neutral value contract for generated gameplay authoring: RuntimeSceneGameplayInteractionSourceRow rows carry action_id, RuntimeSceneGameplayInteractionKind, source_binding_id, target_binding_id, objective_id, and amount; RuntimeSceneGameplayInteractionPlanRequest carries the current RuntimeSceneGameplaySessionState; and plan_runtime_scene_gameplay_interactions consumes resolved RuntimeSceneGameplayBindingRow values to produce deterministic RuntimeSceneGameplayInteractionPlan rows for trigger, pickup, damage, heal, objective_progress, objective_complete, win, loss, and restart interactions. Diagnostics cover invalid or duplicate action ids, missing source or target bindings, missing objective ids, invalid positive amount requirements, and rejected terminal-state transitions; failed plans clear partial rows and preserve the caller-provided session state. This is not a gameplay system scheduler, script runtime, physics/event dispatcher, editor command surface, renderer/RHI execution path, package format change, or broad generated-game production readiness claim.

Runtime Session Profile Path Policy v1 is a backend-neutral value contract for generated game save/settings/profile placement: RuntimeSessionProfilePathRequest carries a safe game_id, profile_id, and project-relative root_path; plan_runtime_session_profile_paths returns deterministic save.gesave, settings.settings, and input.geinputprofile paths under one game-local profile root; and RuntimeSessionProfilePathPlan diagnostics fail closed for invalid game ids, profile ids, root paths, absolute paths, parent traversal, path separators in ids, and control characters. This is not a platform user-directory resolver, cloud save system, binary save format, native handle surface, filesystem mutation helper, or broad save migration/backup workflow.

Runtime Session Profile Document Bundle v1 layers deterministic save/settings/input-profile document load and reset/write helpers over Runtime Session Profile Path Policy v1: RuntimeSessionProfileDocuments carries RuntimeSaveData, RuntimeSettings, and RuntimeInputRebindingProfile values; load_runtime_session_profile_documents returns RuntimeSessionProfileDocumentRow rows with loaded, defaulted_missing, failed_corrupt, failed_unsupported_version, and failed_invalid_path statuses; and write_runtime_session_profile_documents validates all three documents before writing reviewed save.gesave, settings.settings, and input.geinputprofile defaults. Missing documents default without blocking, corrupt and unsupported-version documents block before gameplay startup, and unrelated files under the profile root are not deleted. This is not a cloud save system, binary save format, platform user-directory resolver, save migration/backup workflow, filesystem deletion helper, native handle surface, or game-specific save schema.

Runtime Session Profile Resume Plan v1 reviews already loaded profile documents before selected package resume claims: RuntimeSessionProfileResumeRequest carries a RuntimeSessionProfileDocumentLoadResult plus expected save slot, progression checkpoint, selected package id, and persisted profile id; RuntimeSessionProfileResumePlan reports status, loaded/defaulted document counts, save/settings schema versions, and RuntimeSessionProfileResumeDiagnostic rows; and plan_runtime_session_profile_resume fails closed for blocking document rows, missing save.slot/progression.checkpoint/package.id evidence, and mismatched save-slot/progression/package/profile values. sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package and sample_generated_desktop_runtime_3d_package expose selected --require-runtime-profile-resume counters including runtime_profile_resume_ready, runtime_profile_resume_loaded_documents, runtime_profile_resume_defaulted_documents, runtime_profile_resume_save_schema_version, and runtime_profile_resume_settings_schema_version. This is not a platform user-directory resolver, cloud save system, binary save format, save migration executor, filesystem mutation helper, native handle surface, or game-specific save schema beyond the reviewed resume evidence keys.

Runtime Simulation Persistence v1 reviews already-loaded RuntimeSessionProfileDocuments before simulation resume claims: RuntimeSimulationPersistenceRequest carries document load evidence, expected save slot/world id, current/minimum schema versions, required entity ids, supported migration steps, and key names; RuntimeSimulationPersistencePlan returns RuntimeSimulationPersistenceStatus, RuntimeSimulationPersistentEntityRow values from entity.<id>.(type|region|state_hash), RuntimeSimulationPersistenceMigrationStep rows, RuntimeSimulationPersistenceDiagnostic rows, and RuntimeSimulationPersistenceRemediationAction recommendations; and plan_runtime_simulation_persistence fails closed for blocking save document rows, corrupt save documents, missing save.slot/world.id/snapshot.id/world.tick evidence, malformed or incomplete entity rows, missing required entities, unsupported schema versions, invalid migration rows, and missing migration-chain steps. Non-save profile document failures do not block valid save recovery. This is not a cloud save system, cross-device sync, multiplayer replication layer, binary compatibility policy, platform user-directory resolver, migration execution helper, filesystem/package mutation helper, native handle surface, or game-specific component serializer beyond reviewed save keys.

Runtime Menu HUD Intent Model v1 is a host-independent value contract for generated game HUD/menu intent: RuntimeMenuHudRowDesc rows carry label, counter, prompt, dialogue_box, input_binding_prompt, or command row intent; RuntimeMenuHudCommandIntent and RuntimeMenuHudCommandTarget encode pause/resume/restart/menu/custom commands before game code executes them; and plan_runtime_menu_hud returns deterministic RuntimeMenuHudDisplayRow and RuntimeMenuHudCommandRow rows only when validation succeeds. Diagnostics cover missing or duplicate row ids, unsupported row kinds, missing or duplicate command ids, invalid command intents, and invalid command targets; failed plans clear display and command rows. sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package and sample_generated_desktop_runtime_3d_package expose selected --require-runtime-menu-hud counters including runtime_menu_hud_ready, runtime_menu_hud_display_rows, runtime_menu_hud_command_rows, runtime_menu_hud_dialogue_rows, and runtime_menu_hud_input_binding_prompt_rows. This is not a widget toolkit, UI renderer, focus/navigation system, command execution dispatcher, full input rebinding panel, Dear ImGui/editor surface, platform UI service, native handle surface, or game-specific menu schema.

Runtime Gameplay Debug Overlay v1 is a host-independent value contract for generated gameplay diagnostics: RuntimeGameplayDebugOverlayRowDesc rows carry deterministic row id, RuntimeGameplayDebugOverlayCategory, RuntimeGameplayDebugOverlayRowKind, label, value, highlighted, and visible fields for gameplay, input, audio, physics, navigation, AI, package, session, or custom rows; and plan_runtime_gameplay_debug_overlay returns RuntimeGameplayDebugOverlayPlan rows only when validation succeeds. Diagnostics through RuntimeGameplayDebugOverlayDiagnosticCode and RuntimeGameplayDebugOverlayDiagnostic cover missing or duplicate row ids, missing labels, unsupported categories, and unsupported row kinds; failed plans clear overlay rows. sample_2d_playable_foundation adopts the planner for gameplay/input/audio/session rows and reports debug_overlay_rows=12 in the source-tree smoke. This is not a widget toolkit, UI renderer, renderer/RHI execution path, focus/navigation system, input binding system, debug command dispatcher, Dear ImGui/editor surface, platform UI service, native handle surface, telemetry backend, package streaming path, or game-specific debug schema.

Runtime Input Context Stack Planner v1 is a host-independent value contract for generated game input context handoff: RuntimeInputContextStackRequest carries ordered RuntimeInputContextLayerDesc rows for gameplay, menu, dialogue, rebinding, capture, or overlay layers; plan_runtime_input_context_stack validates context names, rejects duplicate contexts, returns a RuntimeInputContextStackPlan with a RuntimeInputContextStack only after diagnostics pass, supports default-context fallback, lets modal rows block lower-priority layers, and reports gameplay_input_available, gameplay_input_consumed, ui_context_active, and capture_context_active evidence. sample_2d_playable_foundation demonstrates source-tree adoption by planning a passive HUD overlay plus gameplay stack before evaluating RuntimeInputActionMap. Diagnostics cover invalid_context, duplicate_context, and no_active_context; failed plans clear partial stack rows. This is not a UI focus/navigation system, input binding document format change, command dispatcher, platform adapter, native handle surface, full runtime rebinding panel, or game-specific menu schema.

Runtime Script Sandbox Execution Facade v1 is a host-independent reviewed script dispatch contract for generated game-local scripts and mods: RuntimeScriptSandboxPolicyDesc carries module rows, entrypoints, requested permissions, reviewed host API allow-lists, per-entrypoint instruction and memory caps, and explicit replay seeds; plan_runtime_script_sandbox returns deterministic entrypoint and permission plan rows only when diagnostics pass; RuntimeScriptExecutionRequest plus execute_runtime_script_entrypoint dispatch one reviewed planned entrypoint through a caller-owned IRuntimeScriptExecutionAdapter. Execution diagnostics fail closed for missing plans, unplanned policies, missing entrypoints, replay seed mismatches, invalid or over-cap budgets, denied permission rows, adapter failures, and post-dispatch budget traps, and successful executions emit stats, host API call rows, output rows, dispatch evidence, and deterministic replay signatures. This is not a script interpreter, Lua/WASM/JIT runtime, native plugin ABI, filesystem/network/process adapter, package script execution path, renderer/RHI execution path, package format change, editor command surface, or broad modding system.

Runtime Networking Foundation Policy v1 is a host-independent value contract for reviewed multiplayer intent: RuntimeNetworkFoundationPolicyDesc carries network session rows, reviewed transport capability rows, local role, trust boundary, replication channel declarations, and deterministic replay prerequisites; plan_runtime_network_foundation returns deterministic session, transport, channel, and replay prerequisite rows only when diagnostics pass. Diagnostics fail closed for missing or duplicate session/channel ids, unsupported transport capabilities, platform socket or native transport handle requests, insecure untrusted remote sessions without encrypted_transport and authenticated_peer requirements, missing replay seeds, zero fixed replay tick rates, missing deterministic simulation acknowledgement, and missing ordered input acknowledgement. sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package now exposes selected --require-networking-foundation-policy package evidence through networking_foundation_* counters for reviewed session rows, transport rows, channel rows, replay prerequisite rows, rejected unsafe transport requirements, insecure remote transport diagnostics, and clean reviewed-policy diagnostics. This is not a socket or network I/O path, transport middleware, encryption/authentication handshake implementation, rollback/prediction system, matchmaking/NAT traversal implementation, package format change, renderer/RHI path, native handle surface, package mutation, or broad multiplayer readiness claim.

Runtime Network Transport Adapter Facade v1 exposes RuntimeNetworkLoopbackExchangeRequest, RuntimeNetworkLoopbackExchangeResult, RuntimeNetworkPacketDesc, RuntimeNetworkReceivedPacketRow, IRuntimeNetworkTransportAdapter, and execute_runtime_network_loopback_exchange for one reviewed loopback-only exchange through caller-owned adapters. It fails closed for missing or unavailable adapters, unsupported loopback, reliable, or unreliable capabilities, native-handle requests, peer_count != 1, invalid channel counts or channels, payloads over the first-party ceiling, invalid service budgets, and adapter exceptions before dispatch. MK_runtime_network_enet is an optional network-enet/vcpkg adapter proof over ENet loopback only. This is not external network readiness, socket ownership for generated games, encryption/authentication, matchmaking/NAT traversal, replication, rollback, package mutation, native handle exposure, or broad multiplayer readiness.

Runtime Network Production Security Gate v1 exposes RuntimeNetworkProductionThreatModelEvidenceRow, RuntimeNetworkProductionValidationEvidenceRow, RuntimeNetworkUnsupportedOnlineClaimRow, RuntimeNetworkProductionSecurityRequest, RuntimeNetworkProductionSecurityPlan, RuntimeNetworkProductionSecurityDiagnosticCode, and plan_runtime_network_production_security_gate for value-only review of threat-modeled networking evidence. The gate consumes already-reviewed networking foundation, production replication, and loopback exchange results, requires sequence/replay rejection, input command validation, snapshot validation, and rollback-window diagnostic evidence, returns host_evidence_required when only loopback/replication host proof is missing, rejects unsupported encryption/authentication, matchmaking, NAT traversal, cloud service, internet execution, and broad readiness claims, and records zero external network/thread/save/world side effects. sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package --require-gameplay-systems emits network_production_security_* counters with reviewed threat-model evidence, host-gated loopback/replication readiness, zero unsupported online claims, and a positive replay hash. This is not secure transport, peer authentication, matchmaking, NAT traversal, cloud backend services, internet-facing networking, rollback execution, native handle access, or broad online readiness.

Runtime Simulation Orchestration v1 is a host-independent value contract for replay-ready generated gameplay loops: RuntimeSimulationOrchestrationRequest carries simulation_id, next_tick, fixed_tick_rate_hz, accumulated_time_us, max_steps_per_frame, and caller-owned RuntimeSimulationInputCommandDesc rows; plan_runtime_simulation_orchestration returns deterministic fixed-step rows, ordered input-command playback rows, available/planned step counts, consumed/remaining time, and budget_limited status only when diagnostics pass. Diagnostics fail closed for missing simulation ids, invalid fixed tick rates, invalid max steps per frame, missing or duplicate command ids, missing action ids, commands before next_tick, and commands beyond the planned window. sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package now exposes selected --require-simulation-orchestration package evidence through simulation_orchestration_* counters for planned fixed-step rows, ordered command playback rows, budget-limited frames, invalid command diagnostics, consumed/remaining time, and clean reviewed-loop diagnostics. This is not a game-rule scheduler, renderer frame pacing system, networking rollback or lockstep layer, package mutation path, editor runtime host, background worker, platform timing API, native handle surface, or broad deterministic multiplayer readiness claim.

Production Authoring Workflows v1 exposes ProductionAuthoringWorkflowRequest, ProductionAuthoringWorkflowRow, ProductionAuthoringWorkflowReviewResult, and review_production_authoring_workflow so reviewed authoring hosts can validate scene placement, quest/dialogue, item/economy, AI behavior, world region, and validation repair workflow rows before game-owned edits or operator validation. The review returns accepted workflow rows, mutation ledger rows, validation repair rows, and diagnostics only; selected sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package --require-production-authoring-workflows smoke emits production_authoring_workflow_* counters for six accepted workflows, six mutation-ledger rows, one validation-repair row, fail-closed shared-surface/arbitrary-shell/cooked-package/native-backend/non-game-target diagnostics, and zero invoked file mutation, package IO, or command execution. This is value-only review metadata, not file mutation, cooked package mutation, arbitrary shell execution, editor action execution, renderer/RHI work, parser/compiler/native handle exposure, autonomous content generation, or broad production authoring automation.

Runtime UI Workbench Production v1 exposes RuntimeUiWorkbenchDocument, RuntimeUiWorkbenchPanelRow, RuntimeUiWorkbenchTableRow, RuntimeUiWorkbenchGraphSeries, RuntimeUiWorkbenchItemRow, RuntimeUiWorkbenchTextInputFieldRow, RuntimeUiWorkbenchFocusPlan, RuntimeUiWorkbenchLocalizationRef, RuntimeUiWorkbenchAccessibilityRef, RuntimeUiWorkbenchPlan, and plan_runtime_ui_workbench so generated games can review dense menu, inventory, equipment, shop, simulation dashboard, table, graph, focus, text-input, localization, and accessibility intent rows before adapter work. The planner returns deterministic rows only when diagnostics pass and fail-closed diagnostics cover duplicate ids, empty or colliding graph/item/text/focus ids, unknown panel/table/focus references, invalid graph points, invalid item/equipment/shop/text-input rows, missing localization/accessibility metadata, and bounded backend/native/editor/RHI/UI-middleware tokens. Selected sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package --require-runtime-ui-workbench smoke emits runtime_ui_workbench_* counters for five panels, three table columns, two table rows, two graph series, three item rows, inventory/equipment/shop rows, one text-input row, one platform text-input request, four focus edges, seventeen localization refs, localization identity readiness, eleven accessibility refs, accessibility identity readiness, zero renderer/text-shaping/font-rasterization/IME/accessibility-bridge/image-decoding/native-platform adapter invocations, and clean diagnostics. This is value-only UI intent and package evidence, not renderer submission, text shaping, font rasterization, IME session execution, OS accessibility publication, image decoding, renderer texture upload, native handle exposure, Dear ImGui/SDL3/UI middleware coupling, or broad production text/font/accessibility readiness.

Asset Placeholder Generation v1 exposes plan_placeholder_asset_bundle and plan_placeholder_asset_cook_package through placeholder_asset_tool.hpp so reviewed engine tooling can derive deterministic first-party placeholder texture, mesh, material, and audio source documents, synchronized GameEngine.SourceAssetRegistry.v1 content, changed-file hashes, provenance rows, and registered source cook/package planning through PlaceholderAssetBundleRequest, PlaceholderAssetBundlePlan, PlaceholderAssetCookPackageRequest, PlaceholderAssetCookPackagePlan, PlaceholderAssetChangedFile, PlaceholderAssetProvenanceRow, and PlaceholderAssetDiagnostic. Selected replacement recooks preserve stable AssetKeyV2 rows, source revisions, content hashes, provenance/license rows, existing source registry/package index handoff, and package handoff counters through game.agent.json placeholderAssetPipeline replacementWorkflow rows for sprite, mesh, material, audio, UI, and scene-prop placeholder coverage. Diagnostics fail closed for unsafe registry/source/imported paths, duplicate asset keys, unsupported asset kinds, invalid texture dimensions, and invalid audio/material shapes. This is not an external asset downloader, image/audio generation service, broad source importer execution path, renderer/RHI residency path, package streaming path, game-specific art direction system, native handle surface, or broad generated-game production readiness claim.

Sprite Atlas Authoring v1 exposes SpriteAtlasSourcePivot, SpriteAtlasSourceSliceBorder, SpriteAtlasSourcePagePolicyDesc, SpriteAtlasSourceFrameDesc, SpriteAtlasSourceAuthoringDesc, SpriteAtlasSourceAuthoringPlan, SpriteAtlasSourceChangedFile, SpriteAtlasSourceFrameRow, SpriteAtlasSourceAuthoringDiagnostic, and plan_sprite_atlas_source_authoring through sprite_atlas_tool.hpp so reviewed generated 2D host/tooling workflows can validate already-decoded RGBA8 source frames, canonicalize frames by frame id, enforce a selected single-page-tight-rgba8-texture-source page policy, pack through deterministic-sprite-atlas-rgba8-max-side, emit GameEngine.TextureSource.v1 atlas source content, synchronize one GameEngine.SourceAssetRegistry.v1 texture row, and return changed-file hashes plus frame page id/index, UV, pivot, slice-border, and provenance rows. Diagnostics fail closed for unsafe registry/source/imported paths, unsupported page policy, invalid page id, invalid or duplicate frame ids, unsafe frame source paths, unsupported pixel formats, invalid dimensions, byte-count mismatches, invalid normalized pivots, invalid normalized slice borders, atlas max-side overflow, source-registry conflicts, and unsupported runtime_source_image_decoding, renderer_rhi_residency, package_streaming, animation_semantics, editor_productization, and free_form_edit requests. This is not runtime source image decoding, renderer/RHI residency, package streaming, multi-page atlas paging, padded atlas pages, production sprite batching, animation semantics, editor productization, free-form art editing, or game-specific art direction.

Sprite Batching Renderer v1 exposes SpriteBatchBudgetLane, SpriteBatchBudgetProfileStatus, SpriteBatchBudgetDiagnosticCode, SpriteBatchBudgetDesc, SpriteBatchBudgetLanePlanDesc, SpriteBatchBudgetRow, SpriteBatchBudgetDiagnostic, SpriteBatchBudgetProfile, and plan_sprite_batch_budget_profile so reviewed renderer/gameplay workflows can evaluate world, UI, and effects lane budgets over existing SpriteBatchPlan rows. Diagnostics fail closed for missing plans, invalid budgets, plan diagnostics, sprite budget excess, draw budget excess, and texture-bind budget excess. sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package reports sprite_batch_budget_status, sprite_batch_budget_profiles_ready, sprite_batch_budget_rows, sprite_batch_budget_diagnostics, sprite_batch_budget_total_sprites, sprite_batch_budget_total_draws, sprite_batch_budget_total_texture_binds, per-lane ready counters, and per-lane max budget counters alongside existing sprite_batch_plan_* and native_2d_sprite_batch_* package evidence. This is selected plan/budget/native-execution evidence only; material policy, transparency/sorting quality, high-density performance proof, backend renderer-quality gates, Metal readiness, and broad production sprite batching readiness remain follow-up work.

Foundation-only Asset Identity v2 authoring/reference surface is ready for the Engine 1.0 identity boundary: GameEngine.AssetIdentity.v2 stable keys, deterministic validation, AssetId derivation, text IO, GameEngine.SourceAssetRegistry.v1 source registration, plan_asset_identity_placements_v2 production placement rows, Reviewed command-owned apply surfaces, Scene v2 runtime package migration placement_rows, ContentBrowserState Asset Identity labels, SourceAssetRegistryDocumentV1 population through ContentBrowserState::refresh_from, retained content_browser_import.assets key/source labels, GameEngine.Project.v4 project.source_registry, refresh_content_browser_from_project_source_registry, retained Reload Source Registry rows for future visible shell loading, audit_runtime_scene_asset_identity runtime-scene provenance rows, and AssetKeyV2 key-first committed game plus tools/new-game.ps1 template references are implemented. Runtime/cooked/render/UI payloads still consume validated AssetId; runtime source registry parsing, renderer/RHI residency, package streaming, native handles, and 2D/3D vertical slices remain outside this identity closeout.

Cooked metadata-only GameEngine.UiAtlas.v1 document validation/serialization is implemented in MK_assets, and MK_tools exposes author_cooked_ui_atlas_metadata, write_cooked_ui_atlas_metadata, verify_cooked_ui_atlas_package_metadata, PackedUiAtlasAuthoringDesc, author_packed_ui_atlas_from_decoded_images, plan_packed_ui_atlas_package_update, apply_packed_ui_atlas_package_update, PackedUiGlyphAtlasAuthoringDesc, author_packed_ui_glyph_atlas_from_rasterized_glyphs, plan_packed_ui_glyph_atlas_package_update, and apply_packed_ui_glyph_atlas_package_update for deterministic AssetKind::ui_atlas package artifacts, GameEngine.CookedTexture.v1 page artifacts, UiAtlasMetadataImage rows, UiAtlasMetadataGlyph rows, RuntimeUiAtlasGlyph rows, and ui_atlas_texture dependency rows. Runtime source parsing, SVG/vector parsing, renderer texture upload, real font loading/rasterization, text shaping, public native/RHI handles, and broad renderer quality remain unsupported.

Cooked metadata-only GameEngine.Tilemap.v1 document validation/serialization is implemented in MK_assets, MK_tools exposes author_cooked_tilemap_metadata, write_cooked_tilemap_metadata, verify_cooked_tilemap_package_metadata, plan_cooked_tilemap_package_update, and apply_cooked_tilemap_package_update for deterministic AssetKind::tilemap package artifacts and tilemap_texture dependency rows, and MK_runtime inspection validates the atlas texture row, path, and dependency edge. Runtime visible-cell sampling and editor package diagnostics are implemented through the later tilemap runtime UX slice; runtime image decoding and production atlas/editor/runtime integration beyond the reviewed source-image decode, atlas-packing tools, and narrow runtime diagnostics, full tilemap editor UX, native GPU sprite batching, package streaming execution, public native/RHI handles, Metal readiness, and renderer quality remain unsupported or host-gated.

packageSurfaces.notes: Default source-tree build/test/sample lane for headless generated games and engine validation.
Manifest-derived config-only package scaffold with NullRenderer fallback.
Manifest-derived cooked scene package scaffold with runtime package load and renderer-neutral scene submission.

Generated material/shader scaffold with D3D12 host-built shader artifacts and shader_generation_cache_* counters on supported Windows hosts; the strict Vulkan variant proves packaged SPIR-V artifacts plus spirv-val-backed cache/provenance rows only when explicitly gated and does not claim Vulkan renderer residency.
Android package templates and host validation exist on configured hosts, but generated games must run mobile-check and selected Android package commands before claiming readiness.
iOS and Metal readiness requires macOS/Xcode/Apple validation and must remain gated on non-Apple hosts.

physicsBackendAdapterDecisions.decision: Physics 1.0 ready-surface work remains first-party MK_physics only; the optional physics-jolt lane may build MK_physics_jolt, but generated-game defaults and 1.0 ready claims must not require Jolt, native middleware, or public native physics handles.
physicsBackendAdapterDecisions.futureGate: The optional native-physics-middleware-adapter-v1 lane landed through PR #209 / merge commit ccce45701ff3a361977d6286affd46f0ebc16f0d and owns the vcpkg manifest feature physics-jolt, tools/validate-physics-jolt.ps1, docs/dependencies.md, docs/legal-and-licensing.md, THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md, first-party facade types, deterministic conversion/replay/budget tests, and fail-closed capability negotiation. Any broader middleware parity, cross-platform determinism claim, generated-game default adoption, or public native-handle interop still requires a new dated plan with benchmark and host evidence.
unsupportedClaims: Jolt runtime integration in the default/generated-game 1.0 ready surface
native physics handles in public gameplay APIs
dependency-free 1.0 ready surface requiring third-party physics

hostGates.notes: D3D12 is the primary currently verified Windows visible/package lane, but gameplay APIs still must not expose D3D12 or RHI handles.

hosts: host time budget for 108000-frame package soak
hostGates.notes: Long-run soak execution is explicitly opt-in and outside default validation. It must be acknowledged before Execute mode and must not promote broad long-running readiness, Linux affinity, NUMA execution, GPU async overlap, CUDA/HIP/SYCL, cross-vendor parity, cross-backend parity, or broad CPU/GPU/memory optimization.

hosts: Windows Intel or AMD host with Windows Performance Toolkit
Linux Intel or AMD host with perf
Intel host with VTune Profiler when selected
AMD host with AMD uProf when selected
hostGates.notes: CPU profiling matrix evidence is external host-owned artifact collection. Missing profiler tools, missing PMU support, incomplete topology, missing NUMA/NPS state, missing before/after traces, or absent regression budgets must report host-gated evidence rather than ready CPU optimization.

hosts: Windows D3D12 or Vulkan capable host with GPU profiler
Linux Vulkan capable host with GPU profiler
NVIDIA CUDA Toolkit host when selected
hostGates.notes: Optional GPU compute review evidence is external host-owned artifact collection. Missing GPU profiler, missing stream/event proof, missing queue synchronization, missing CUDA/HIP/SYCL runtime, missing SYCL backend, or missing scalar/RHI fallback evidence must remain host-gated and must not introduce default validation, vcpkg.json, CMake, runtime dependency, broad GPU compute, async overlap, cross-vendor parity, cross-backend parity, or broad CPU/GPU/memory optimization claims.
Vulkan package/visible claims require explicit strict smoke commands and local runtime/toolchain readiness. Do not generalize Windows proof to other hosts.
Metal visible presentation and Apple package readiness remain Apple-host gated and must not be marked ready from Windows validation.

hosts: configured signing and AVD/device when required
hostGates.notes: Android Debug/Release/signing/emulator lanes depend on mobile-check output and selected game package commands.
The optional native MK_editor lane is Windows-only and validates Win32/first-party retained UI/Direct3D 12 smoke behavior without SDL3, UI middleware, vcpkg UI packages, or public native handles.

hosts: WIN32 CMake targets MK_editor_game_module_driver_probe and MK_editor_game_module_driver_load_tests
hostGates.notes: Windows-only editor game module driver reload transaction CTest lane requires local MSVC dev preset build outputs for the probe shared library and load-test executable. Does not launch mirakana_editor or claim active-session hot reload or stable third-party ABI.

hosts: default Windows Visual Studio dev preset through CMake File API codemodel
Makefile or Ninja generators with native compile_commands.json
hostGates.notes: Strict clang-tidy compile database availability is ready for the default dev validation path. tools/build.ps1 prepares CMake File API codemodel data before the dev configure/build, tools/validate.ps1 reuses that reply for the check-tidy.ps1 -MaxFiles 1 smoke and then runs test.ps1 -SkipBuild, while standalone tools/check-tidy.ps1 still configures the preset when native compile_commands.json is unavailable. tools/build.ps1 and tools/test.ps1 default to automatic CMake/CTest parallelism through official CMake/CTest --parallel options and accept -Jobs <N> only when a local host needs an explicit lower throttle; time-sensitive host-resource tests use CTest RUN_SERIAL rather than disabling suite-wide parallelism. The wrapper synthesizes clang-tidy arguments rows for Visual Studio generator builds, supports -Files for targeted changed-file clang-tidy on compile-database source files, and still reports missing CMake or clang-tidy as local tool blockers.

toolSurfaces.writeScope: Generated-game tasks write game-owned roots by default; shared engine, tooling, schema, docs, rules, skill, and manifest surfaces require a developer-owned capability task.
Generated-game tasks write game-owned roots by default; shared engine, tooling, schema, docs, rules, skill, and manifest surfaces require a developer-owned capability task.
Generated-game tasks write game-owned roots by default; shared engine, tooling, schema, docs, rules, skill, and manifest surfaces require a developer-owned capability task.

gameOwnedWriteScopes: game-local source assets and runtime package files declared by game.agent.json
game-local validation evidence under configured evidence roots
sharedSurfaceWritePolicy: Engine, editor, renderer, tools, schemas, docs, rules, skills, subagents, and manifest fragments require a developer-owned capability task and isolated topic branch.
Default branch direct push is forbidden; publish task-owned changes through a PR.
Do not add broad permission grants for raw shells, package managers, force pushes, destructive cleanup, immediate PR merge, or raw gh pr ready.
Use linked-worktree preparation and guarded post-merge cleanup for isolated production tasks.
forbiddenBroadGrants: force push without explicit branch-owned request
arbitrary shell or raw manifest command evaluation
credential or secret storage in repo
unsupportedClaims: autonomous engine-internal edits from game generation

documentationPolicy.copyingPolicy: Use documentation for guidance; do not copy external code unless the license is explicit and recorded.

sourcePolicy.vcpkgConfigurePolicy: CMake configure must not install, restore, or download vcpkg packages; vcpkg-enabled presets use VCPKG_MANIFEST_INSTALL=OFF and VCPKG_INSTALLED_DIR=${sourceDir}/vcpkg_installed.

This is a narrow generated 3D cooked glyph metadata smoke; production text shaping, font rasterization, glyph atlas generation, runtime source image decoding, source image atlas packing, Metal parity, native handles, and broad generated 3D production readiness remain unsupported.
gameCodeGuidance.desktopRuntime3dPlayablePackageSmoke: DesktopRuntime3DPackage scaffolds and the committed sample can require --require-playable-3d-slice on selected installed package smokes that already request package streaming, scene GPU bindings, postprocess, and renderer quality gates. The smoke reports playable_3d_status, playable_3d_ready, playable_3d_diagnostics, playable_3d_expected_frames, playable_3d_scene_mesh_plan_ready, playable_3d_camera_controller_ready, playable_3d_animation_ready, playable_3d_morph_ready, playable_3d_quaternion_ready, playable_3d_package_streaming_ready, playable_3d_scene_gpu_ready, playable_3d_postprocess_ready, playable_3d_renderer_quality_ready, and selected compute readiness fields through first-party status output. This is a narrow generated 3D playable package smoke; runtime source import, broad dependency cooking, broad/background package streaming, Metal parity, native handles, general renderer quality, and broad generated 3D production readiness remain unsupported.
gameCodeGuidance.currentRenderingBackendParityHostRecipeP: Backend Renderer Parity Host Recipe Proof v1 requires Metal BackendRendererParityProofRow entries to carry reviewed host_validation_recipe_id values from shader-toolchain, mobile-packaging, renderer-metal-apple-host-evidence, or ios-simulator-smoke. plan_backend_renderer_parity_policy reports missing_host_validation_recipe for omitted recipes and unreviewed_host_validation_recipe for arbitrary valid ids, validates recipe ids against native-token leakage, includes them in replay hashes, keeps Metal host-gated without Apple-host proof, and does not transfer D3D12 or Vulkan proof to Metal readiness.
gameCodeGuidance.runtimeMorphMeshGpuBinding: Narrow D3D12 primary-lane GPU morph proof: MK_runtime_rhi exposes upload_runtime_morph_mesh_cpu, make_runtime_morph_mesh_gpu_binding, and attach_morph_mesh_descriptor_set for RuntimeMorphMeshCpuPayload POSITION deltas at morph descriptor binding 0, optional NORMAL/TANGENT deltas at bindings 2/3, and 256-byte weight uniform binding 1; MK_renderer exposes MorphMeshGpuBinding normal_delta_buffer/tangent_delta_buffer metadata, MeshCommand::gpu_morphing, RhiFrameRendererDesc::morph_graphics_pipeline, gpu_morph_draws, and morph_descriptor_binds. Direct D3D12 readback proves graphics-pipeline POSITION deformation and NORMAL/TANGENT lit-output changes; RuntimeMorphMeshComputeBinding and create_runtime_morph_mesh_compute_binding now prove direct D3D12 compute POSITION/NORMAL/TANGENT output readback, make_runtime_compute_morph_output_mesh_gpu_binding proves POSITION renderer consumption, generated DesktopRuntime3DPackage D3D12/Vulkan smoke proves POSITION/NORMAL/TANGENT compute output rendering plus package-visible queue-wait evidence when explicitly requested, make_runtime_compute_morph_skinned_mesh_gpu_binding plus SkinnedMeshGpuBinding::skin_attribute_vertex_buffer prove a D3D12 runtime/renderer skin+compute-morph composition path with a distinct joint-palette descriptor set, MK_runtime_scene_rhi retains RuntimeSceneComputeMorphSkinnedMeshBinding rows in SceneSkinnedGpuBindingPalette through a host-owned runtime scene RHI bridge, and generated DesktopRuntime3DPackage D3D12/Vulkan smoke can now require first-party skin+compute package counters while D3D12 also reports scene_gpu_compute_morph_async_* sequencing telemetry from IRhiDevice::stats(). RHI/D3D12 queue-submit and queue-wait fence sequencing telemetry is covered by runtime-rhi-compute-morph-async-telemetry-d3d12-v1 and generated package promotion is covered by generated-3d-compute-morph-async-telemetry-package-smoke-d3d12-v1. Runtime RHI Compute Morph Async Overlap Evidence D3D12 v1 adds mirakana::rhi::RhiAsyncOverlapReadinessDiagnostics and diagnose_compute_graphics_async_overlap_readiness, which deterministically reports not_proven_serial_dependency for the current same-frame graphics queue wait without exposing native handles, timestamp resources, or package-visible performance claims. Runtime RHI Compute Morph Pipelined Output Ring D3D12 v1 adds RuntimeMorphMeshComputeBindingOptions::output_slot_count, RuntimeMorphMeshComputeOutputSlot, slot-indexed make_runtime_compute_morph_output_mesh_gpu_binding, and D3D12 descriptor-range proof that slot 1 can receive compute-written POSITION output through binding 3 without aliasing slot 0. Runtime RHI Compute Morph Pipelined Scheduling D3D12 v1 adds mirakana::rhi::RhiPipelinedComputeGraphicsScheduleEvidence and diagnose_pipelined_compute_graphics_async_overlap_readiness so first-party diagnostics can classify graphics consuming a previous output slot while compute writes a different current slot as backend-private timing-ready rather than a same-frame serial dependency. RHI D3D12 Per-Queue Fence Synchronization v1 completed first-party FenceValue queue identity and backend-private per-queue D3D12 fence timelines. Vulkan async/performance work, Metal parity, graphics morph+skin composition, and broad generated 3D production readiness remain follow-up work; rhi-d3d12-queue-timestamp-measurement-foundation-v1 is the active RHI timing slice for backend-private graphics/compute timestamp interval diagnostics.
gameCodeGuidance.runtimeSceneRhiMorphGpuPalette: Host-owned package bridge for the GPU morph proof: MK_runtime_scene_rhi accepts RuntimeSceneGpuBindingOptions::morph_mesh_assets, validates selected cooked morph_mesh_cpu records, uploads them through MK_runtime_rhi including optional NORMAL/TANGENT delta streams when present, retains SceneMorphGpuBindingPalette plus morph_descriptor_set_layout and morph_mesh_uploads, reports morph_mesh_uploads/uploaded_morph_bytes, and releases morph buffers/descriptors through execute_runtime_scene_gpu_binding_safe_point_teardown on NullRhiDevice. gameCodeGuidance.currentObservability: MK_core provides dependency-free DiagnosticsRecorder, DiagnosticEvent, CounterSample, ProfileSample, ManualProfileClock, SteadyProfileClock, ScopedProfileZone, summarize_diagnostics, DiagnosticsBudgetThresholds, DiagnosticsBudgetSummary, DiagnosticsBudgetStatus, summarize_counter_budget, summarize_profile_budget, DiagnosticsTraceExportOptions, export_diagnostics_trace_json, DiagnosticsTraceImportReview, DiagnosticsTraceImportResult, review_diagnostics_trace_json, import_diagnostics_trace_json, DiagnosticsOpsHostStatus, DiagnosticsOpsPlanOptions, DiagnosticsOpsArtifact, DiagnosticsOpsPlan, and build_diagnostics_ops_plan for deterministic CPU diagnostics, counters, profile samples, summary aggregation, named nearest-rank p95/p99 budget summaries, Trace Event JSON subset export/review/reconstruction, and operations handoff rows without runtime, renderer, platform, SDL3, editor, OS, GPU, network upload, or Dear ImGui dependencies. summarize_counter_budget and summarize_profile_budget report count/min/average/p95/p99/max, missing-sample diagnostics, invalid non-finite counter diagnostics, invalid-threshold diagnostics, and threshold status before broader optimization claims. build_diagnostics_ops_plan reports ready diagnostic-summary and trace-event-json artifacts, a host_gated crash_dump_review row until Debugging Tools for Windows are available, and unsupported telemetry_upload without a caller-provided backend. review_diagnostics_trace_json structurally reviews pasted supported Trace Event JSON and classifies M/i/C/X/unknown phases; import_diagnostics_trace_json reconstructs only first-party exported M/i/C/X Trace Event JSON fields into DiagnosticCapture rows and reports deterministic diagnostics for unsupported reconstruction fields. MK_runtime_host provides the first host adapter: DesktopHostServices can accept a DiagnosticsRecorder plus optional IProfileClock, and DesktopGameRunner records runtime_host.frame profile samples plus renderer.frames_started, renderer.frames_finished, renderer.sprites_submitted, and renderer.meshes_submitted counters from IRenderer::stats() without exposing native/backend details. MK_editor_core provides a recorder-backed editor profiler model that turns DiagnosticCapture plus editor status counters into deterministic summary, profile, counter, event, trace export, trace file save, native trace save dialog review, telemetry handoff, trace import review with reconstructed capture rows, project-relative trace file import review with reconstructed capture rows, native trace open dialog review, and retained MK_ui rows; MK_editor records editor.frame plus editor counters through DiagnosticsRecorder before adapting those rows to Dear ImGui and owning the SDL-backed Browse Save Trace JSON export dialog and Browse Trace JSON import dialog. MK_rhi exposes backend-neutral GPU debug scope/marker hooks with printable-label validation, RhiStats counters, D3D12 PIX-friendly command-list events, null/Vulkan count-only marker paths, D3D12 graphics-queue timestamp frequency reporting, and read-only IRhiDevice memory_diagnostics snapshots for DXGI video-memory data or descriptor-byte estimates. Native dump writing, crash-report backends, telemetry SDK/upload execution, symbol publication, production allocator hooks/enforcement, timestamp query capture, Vulkan/Metal native debug-label parity, broader editor native save/open dialogs outside Profiler, arbitrary JSON conversion beyond the first-party exported Trace Event JSON subset, and flame graph UI remain follow-up adapters.
gameCodeGuidance.currentMemoryDiagnostics: MK_core Memory Diagnostics v1 and Frame/Thread Scratch v1 provide dependency-free ScratchArena, ScratchLease, ScratchLeaseStatus, scratch_lease_status_label, MemoryLifetimeClass, MemoryCounterRow, MemoryDiagnosticsOptions, MemoryClassDiagnosticsSummary, MemoryDiagnosticsSummary, MemoryBudgetPressure, MemoryDiagnosticsCode, MemoryDiagnosticsStatus, summarize_memory_diagnostics, memory_lifetime_class_label, memory_budget_pressure_label, memory_diagnostics_code_label, and memory_diagnostics_status_label for bounded frame temporary / worker scratch leases and memory evidence by lifetime class. ScratchArena exposes non-owning std::span<std::byte> leases from stable non-copyable/non-movable arena owners, reset_at_safe_point boundaries, owner-checked worker releases, padding-aware capacity/high-water evidence, and memory_counter_row export. The diagnostics model aggregates bytes, allocation counts, reuse counts, reset counts, high-water bytes, budget pressure, invalid counter rows, stale-generation diagnostics, use-after-safe-point diagnostics, cross-thread-free diagnostics, and false-sharing diagnostics before broad allocator or scheduler optimization claims. sample_desktop_runtime_game --require-memory-diagnostics maps selected package resident CPU bytes, resident GPU bytes/budget, upload/staging bytes, transient GPU allocation counters, and framegraph aliasing-barrier evidence into package-visible memory_diagnostics_* counters including memory_diagnostics_status=ready, memory_diagnostics_ready=1, memory_diagnostics_diagnostics=0, positive memory_diagnostics_total_bytes, positive memory_diagnostics_package_resident_cpu_bytes, positive memory_diagnostics_resident_gpu_bytes, positive memory_diagnostics_upload_staging_bytes, memory_diagnostics_resident_gpu_pressure=nominal, positive memory_diagnostics_transient_gpu_aliasing_barriers, and memory_diagnostics_transient_gpu_framegraph_aliasing_ready=1 when multi-queue evidence is requested. It does not replace or enforce global allocators, broad pool policy, all-core scheduling, NUMA/affinity tuning, SIMD dispatch, CUDA/HIP/SYCL paths, automatic/LRU GPU residency, cross-vendor parity, cross-backend parity, or broad memory optimization readiness.
gameCodeGuidance.currentCpuSimdDispatchDiagnostics: MK_core SIMD Dispatch Policy And Evidence v1 provides CpuSimdFeatureSet, CpuSimdLane, CpuSimdLaneRequest, SimdDispatchPolicyDesc, SimdDispatchPolicy, SimdDotProductEvidence, observe_cpu_simd_features, select_simd_dispatch_policy, compute_scalar_dot_product, build_simd_dot_product_evidence, cpu_simd_lane_label, cpu_simd_lane_request_label, simd_dispatch_status_label, and simd_dispatch_diagnostic_code_label. The selected sample_desktop_runtime_game --require-simd-dispatch-policy package smoke reports simd_dispatch_policy_status=ready, simd_dispatch_policy_ready=1, simd_dispatch_policy_requested_lane=auto_select, simd_dispatch_policy_selected_lane=scalar, sse2, or avx2, simd_dispatch_policy_diagnostics=0, simd_dispatch_policy_input_count=8, simd_dispatch_policy_dot_product_result=120, std::span<const float> borrowed inputs, no retained raw pointers, simd_dispatch_policy_reviewed_avx2_target_available, gated simd_dispatch_policy_avx2_selected, and zero native handle, NUMA allocation, GPU async-overlap, CUDA, HIP, and SYCL side-effect counters. Generated games can use this as narrow scalar/SSE2/AVX2 CPU math dispatch evidence only; AVX2 is selected only when the target-local reviewed AVX2 object and runtime AVX register gates pass. ARM NEON, broad Intel/AMD tuning, broad SIMD quality, GPU compute, CUDA/HIP/SYCL, and broad CPU/GPU/memory optimization remain unsupported until separate focused evidence lands.
gameCodeGuidance.currentCpuProfilingMatrix: Intel/AMD CPU Profiling Matrix v1 is exposed through engine/agent/manifest.json.aiOperableProductionLoop.cpuProfilingMatrix as host-gated evidence selection, not runtime tuning. Generated games and engine follow-up work must treat every host class as host-gated until exact CPU model, topology, SMT, processor groups, NUMA/NPS, scheduler context, compiler flags, selected SIMD lane, thermal/power state, profiler name/version, official-tool trace artifacts, before/after trace pairs, and regression budgets are attached. Findings must classify as data-layout candidate, batch-size candidate, scheduler policy candidate, Linux affinity candidate, NUMA placement candidate, SIMD kernel candidate, compiler-lane candidate, or non-goal. The matrix does not apply Linux affinity, NUMA allocation execution, broad SIMD, PGO/LTO, GPU async overlap, CUDA/HIP/SYCL, cross-vendor parity, cross-backend parity, or broad CPU/GPU/memory optimization.
gameCodeGuidance.currentOptionalGpuComputeReview: Optional GPU Compute Review v1 is exposed through engine/agent/manifest.json.aiOperableProductionLoop.optionalGpuComputeReview as review-only evidence selection. Generated games and engine follow-up work must classify every candidate as rhi_compute, offline_tool_acceleration, cuda_hip_private_adapter_candidate, sycl_private_adapter_candidate, or non_goal, and must attach data transfer cost, memory residency, synchronization, stream/event usage, queue/profiler visibility, dependency burden, and scalar or RHI fallback evidence before implementation. Runtime rendering or simulation candidates should prefer backend-neutral RHI compute. CUDA/HIP/SYCL can be considered only for optional tooling or private adapters with explicit install gates and fallback paths. This review does not add a CUDA/HIP/SYCL runtime dependency, vcpkg.json feature, CMake linkage, default validation dependency, broad GPU compute, async overlap, cross-vendor parity, cross-backend parity, public vendor handles, or broad CPU/GPU/memory optimization.
gameCodeGuidance.currentJobSchedulingDiagnostics: MK_core Job Scheduling Evidence v1 Phase 3 provides dependency-free value rows, summaries, deterministic bounded queue execution evidence, and selected package-visible scheduler evidence before broad scheduler execution: JobWorkerTopologyRow, JobQueueCounterRow, JobSchedulingWorkItemRow, JobSchedulingExecutionOptions, JobSchedulingExecutionOrderRow, JobSchedulingExecutionEvidence, JobSchedulingDiagnosticsSummary, JobSchedulingDiagnosticsCode, JobSchedulingDiagnosticsStatus, summarize_job_scheduling_diagnostics, build_job_scheduling_execution_evidence, job_scheduling_diagnostics_code_label, and job_scheduling_diagnostics_status_label. The model aggregates logical processor count, worker count, queue count, processor group count, NUMA node count, explicit dependency ids, worker-local output counts, deterministic merge order, submitted/completed jobs, bounded queue capacity, queue depth high-water, queue overflow count, steal attempts/successes, worker waits, blocked dependencies, dependency cycles, deterministic merge counts, nondeterministic merge counts, per-worker MemoryCounterRow scratch charges, scratch bytes/high-water, scratch misuse counts, undersized job batches, and oversized job batches. build_job_scheduling_execution_evidence returns queue_rows, worker_scratch_rows, execution_order, scheduling_summary, and scratch_summary so generated or sample games can prove deterministic scheduling participation without starting threads. sample_desktop_runtime_game --require-job-scheduling-evidence reports package-visible job_scheduling_evidence_status=ready, job_scheduling_evidence_ready=1, job_scheduling_evidence_diagnostics=0, job_scheduling_evidence_scratch_diagnostics=0, job_scheduling_evidence_worker_count=2, job_scheduling_evidence_queue_rows=2, job_scheduling_evidence_submitted_jobs=3, job_scheduling_evidence_completed_jobs=3, job_scheduling_evidence_execution_rows=3, job_scheduling_evidence_deterministic_merges=3, positive job_scheduling_evidence_scratch_bytes, positive job_scheduling_evidence_scratch_high_water_bytes, job_scheduling_evidence_native_threads_started=0, job_scheduling_evidence_thread_pool_started=0, job_scheduling_evidence_affinity_policy_applied=0, job_scheduling_evidence_numa_policy_applied=0, job_scheduling_evidence_simd_dispatch_applied=0, and job_scheduling_evidence_gpu_async_overlap_applied=0. It fail-closes for missing rows, invalid worker topology, missing processor group evidence, missing NUMA evidence, invalid queue rows, queue overflow, blocked dependencies, dependency cycles, cross-thread scratch misuse through scratch_owner_worker_id, nondeterministic merges including duplicate merge orders, undersized job batches, and oversized job batches. texture-sampling proof remains available when MK_VULKAN_TEST_RUNTIME_MATERIAL_VERTEX_SPV and MK_VULKAN_TEST_RUNTIME_MATERIAL_FRAGMENT_SPV are configured, and environment-gated cooked runtime scene GPU palette proof remains available when MK_VULKAN_TEST_RUNTIME_SCENE_VERTEX_SPV and MK_VULKAN_TEST_RUNTIME_SCENE_FRAGMENT_SPV point at real scene SPIR-V artifacts, and environment-gated compute dispatch readback proof remains available when MK_VULKAN_TEST_COMPUTE_SPV points at a real compute SPIR-V artifact, and environment-gated Runtime RHI compute morph POSITION readback proof remains available when MK_VULKAN_TEST_COMPUTE_MORPH_SPV points at a real compute morph SPIR-V artifact, environment-gated Runtime RHI compute morph renderer-consumption proof remains available when MK_VULKAN_TEST_COMPUTE_MORPH_RENDER_VERTEX_SPV and MK_VULKAN_TEST_COMPUTE_MORPH_RENDER_FRAGMENT_SPV point at real SPIR-V artifacts, and environment-gated Runtime RHI compute morph NORMAL/TANGENT readback proof remains available when MK_VULKAN_TEST_COMPUTE_MORPH_TANGENT_FRAME_SPV points at a real tangent-frame compute SPIR-V artifact. create_runtime_device resolves optional vkSetDebugUtilsObjectNameEXT through vkGetDeviceProcAddr and applies best-effort GameEngine.RHI.Vulkan.* debug object names to the logical device, graphics and present queues, swapchains, images, image views, buffers, command pools, primary command buffers, shader modules, pipeline layouts, graphics pipelines, samplers, and related runtime handles when the ICD exports the entry point (null proc skips naming).
gameCodeGuidance.currentVulkanRuntimeOwners: Vulkan runtime owners now include VulkanRuntimeInstance, VulkanRuntimeDevice, VulkanRuntimeCommandPool, VulkanRuntimeBuffer, VulkanRuntimeTexture, VulkanRuntimeShaderModule, VulkanRuntimeDescriptorSetLayout, VulkanRuntimeDescriptorSet, VulkanRuntimePipelineLayout, VulkanRuntimeGraphicsPipeline, VulkanRuntimeComputePipeline, VulkanRuntimeSwapchain, VulkanRuntimeFrameSync, VulkanRuntimeReadbackBuffer, and VulkanRuntimeSampler. The create_rhi_device bridge exposes backend-neutral BufferHandle, TextureHandle, ShaderHandle, DescriptorSetLayoutHandle, DescriptorSetHandle, PipelineLayoutHandle, GraphicsPipelineHandle, ComputePipelineHandle, SwapchainHandle, SwapchainFrameHandle, graphics command lists, immediate FenceValue stats/waits, upload-buffer writes, descriptor updates for uniform/storage buffer, sampled texture, and sampler bindings, standalone texture transitions, buffer copies, buffer-to-texture copies, texture-to-buffer copies, standalone texture render-target clears, optional depth attachment clears, standalone texture render-target draws with optional depth attachments, copy-only submit, command-list-local texture state tracking, stale texture-state submit rejection, submit-success-only texture-state commits, and transient lease creation while keeping VkBuffer, VkImage, VkDeviceMemory, VkShaderModule, VkDescriptorSetLayout, VkDescriptorPool, VkDescriptorSet, VkWriteDescriptorSet, VkDescriptorBufferInfo, VkDescriptorImageInfo, VkSampler, VkBufferCopy, VkBufferImageCopy, VkPipelineLayout, VkPipeline, VkSurfaceKHR, VkSwapchainKHR, VkImageView, VkSemaphore, VkFence, and native commands private. Descriptor creation, update, and binding use create_runtime_descriptor_set_layout, create_runtime_descriptor_set, update_runtime_descriptor_set, descriptor-aware create_runtime_pipeline_layout, and record_runtime_descriptor_set_binding while VkDescriptorSetLayout, VkDescriptorPool, VkDescriptorSet, VkWriteDescriptorSet, VkDescriptorBufferInfo, vkUpdateDescriptorSets, vkCmdBindDescriptorSets, and Vulkan function pointers remain private to MK_rhi_vulkan. Pipeline creation uses create_runtime_pipeline_layout, create_runtime_graphics_pipeline, and create_runtime_compute_pipeline with dynamic rendering metadata, native DepthStencilStateDesc mapping, and compute shader modules while VkPipelineLayout, VkPipeline, VkPipelineVertexInputStateCreateInfo, VkGraphicsPipelineCreateInfo, VkComputePipelineCreateInfo, vertex input descriptions, and Vulkan function pointers remain private to MK_rhi_vulkan. Swapchain creation uses create_runtime_swapchain while VkSurfaceKHR, VkSwapchainKHR, VkImage, VkImageView, and image-view destruction order remain private to MK_rhi_vulkan. Frame sync, acquire, and present use create_runtime_frame_sync, acquire_next_runtime_swapchain_image, and present_runtime_swapchain_image while VkSemaphore, VkFence, VkPresentInfoKHR, vkAcquireNextImageKHR/vkQueuePresentKHR results, and acquired/presented image indices remain private or first-party mapped; the IRhiDevice bridge records shader-free swapchain clear render passes, queues present, submits through private synchronization2, and completes frame ownership on submit. Dynamic rendering color/depth clear and draw recording use record_runtime_dynamic_rendering_clear, record_runtime_dynamic_rendering_draw, record_runtime_texture_rendering_clear, and record_runtime_texture_rendering_draw while VkRenderingInfo, VkRenderingAttachmentInfo including pDepthAttachment, VkCommandBuffer, VkPipeline, VkImageView, viewport/scissor state, vertex/index buffers, and vkCmdBeginRendering/vkCmdBindPipeline/vkCmdBindVertexBuffers/vkCmdBindIndexBuffer/vkCmdDraw/vkCmdDrawIndexed/vkCmdEndRendering function pointers remain private. Standalone texture transition/copy recording uses build_texture_transition_barrier, record_runtime_texture_barrier, record_runtime_buffer_copy, record_runtime_buffer_texture_copy, and record_runtime_texture_buffer_copy while shader-read hazards, depth_write hazards, D24S8 depth|stencil image-barrier aspects, usage/format/state compatibility checks, VkImageMemoryBarrier2, VkDependencyInfo, VkBufferCopy, VkBufferImageCopy, vkCmdCopyBuffer, vkCmdCopyBufferToImage, vkCmdCopyImageToBuffer, and image layouts remain private or first-party mapped. Synchronization2 recording uses record_runtime_swapchain_frame_barrier and submit_runtime_command_buffer while VkDependencyInfo, VkImageMemoryBarrier2, VkSubmitInfo2, VkSemaphoreSubmitInfo, VkCommandBufferSubmitInfo, queue handles, semaphores, fences, and raw VkResult values remain private or first-party mapped; requested queue-idle waits must succeed before submit_runtime_command_buffer reports submitted, and logical device creation requires the synchronization2 feature before those commands are requested. Upload and readback use write_runtime_buffer, read_runtime_buffer, create_runtime_readback_buffer, record_runtime_swapchain_image_readback, and read_runtime_readback_buffer while VkBuffer, VkDeviceMemory, VkMemoryRequirements, VkPhysicalDeviceMemoryProperties, VkBufferImageCopy, mapped pointers, and native memory commands remain private or first-party mapped. Environment-gated visible draw/readback, raw texture-sampling, depth-ordered draw/readback, sampled-depth readback, height-fog runtime readback, physical-sky runtime readback, shadow receiver readback, RhiFrameRenderer runtime material texture-sampling, and cooked runtime scene GPU palette proofs now run through IRhiDevice when MK_VULKAN_TEST_VERTEX_SPV/MK_VULKAN_TEST_FRAGMENT_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_TEXTURE_VERTEX_SPV/MK_VULKAN_TEST_TEXTURE_FRAGMENT_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_DEPTH_VERTEX_SPV/MK_VULKAN_TEST_DEPTH_FRAGMENT_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_DEPTH_SAMPLE_VERTEX_SPV/MK_VULKAN_TEST_DEPTH_SAMPLE_FRAGMENT_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_POSTPROCESS_DEPTH_VERTEX_SPV/MK_VULKAN_TEST_POSTPROCESS_DEPTH_FRAGMENT_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_HEIGHT_FOG_DEPTH_VERTEX_SPV/MK_VULKAN_TEST_HEIGHT_FOG_DEPTH_FRAGMENT_SPV plus MK_VULKAN_TEST_HEIGHT_FOG_VERTEX_SPV/MK_VULKAN_TEST_HEIGHT_FOG_FRAGMENT_SPV and VK_LAYER_KHRONOS_validation, MK_VULKAN_TEST_PHYSICAL_SKY_VERTEX_SPV/MK_VULKAN_TEST_PHYSICAL_SKY_FRAGMENT_SPV plus VK_LAYER_KHRONOS_validation, MK_VULKAN_TEST_SHADOW_RECEIVER_VERTEX_SPV/MK_VULKAN_TEST_SHADOW_RECEIVER_FRAGMENT_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_RUNTIME_MATERIAL_VERTEX_SPV/MK_VULKAN_TEST_RUNTIME_MATERIAL_FRAGMENT_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_COMPUTE_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_COMPUTE_MORPH_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_COMPUTE_MORPH_RENDER_VERTEX_SPV/MK_VULKAN_TEST_COMPUTE_MORPH_RENDER_FRAGMENT_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_COMPUTE_MORPH_TANGENT_FRAME_SPV, or MK_VULKAN_TEST_RUNTIME_SCENE_VERTEX_SPV/MK_VULKAN_TEST_RUNTIME_SCENE_FRAGMENT_SPV point at real SPIR-V artifacts and the local Vulkan runtime is available; default generated-game availability remains gated by runtime and shader-toolchain readiness.
gameCodeGuidance.currentMetal: SDK-independent MK_rhi_metal scaffold exposes backend identity, host support, capability profile, probe plan, deterministic probe result helpers, non-empty metallib shader library artifact validation, runtime/default-device/command-queue/shader-library readiness planning, texture synchronization planning for render/blit/present encoder intent, platform availability diagnostics, MetalEnvironmentFeatureHostEvidenceDesc, MetalEnvironmentFeatureEvidenceRequirement with synchronization_evidence_required, MetalEnvironmentFeatureEvidenceRow, MetalEnvironmentFeatureHostEvidencePlan, default_environment_feature_evidence_requirements, build_environment_feature_host_evidence_plan, and metal_environment_feature_host_evidence_status_line for feature-local physical_sky, height_fog, cloud_layer, precipitation, volumetric_fog, volumetric_cloud, and environment_lighting_ibl Apple host-gated evidence rows tied to renderer-metal-apple-host-evidence without promoting Metal readiness from Windows/D3D12/Vulkan proof or exposing native handles. Apple-only native paths expose MetalNativeEnvironmentFeatureHostEvidenceDesc, MetalNativeEnvironmentFeatureHostEvidenceResult, and create_native_environment_feature_host_evidence behind private Objective-C++ implementation; the reviewed recipe builds environment_feature_evidence.metallib from MSL, loads it as an MTLLibrary, creates render and compute pipeline states, creates cube HDR and depth/render target textures plus a particle buffer, executes command buffers, proves render and compute readback_nonzero, emits metal_environment_* counters, and keeps native_handle_access=0. The same Apple-only layer continues default-device, command-queue, command-buffer, render-encoder, texture-target, CAMetalLayer drawable acquisition, drawable render-encoder creation, drawable present scheduling, command-buffer submission, and texture-readback ownership through retained private PIMPL objects without including Metal or QuartzCore headers or exposing native handles. Apple Objective-C++ paths assign GameEngine.RHI.Metal.* GPU debugger labels through -[MTLObject setLabel:] on the default MTLDevice, MTLCommandQueue, per-allocation MTLCommandBuffer, offscreen and drawable-backed MTLTexture targets, MTLRenderCommandEncoder, and transient readback MTLCommandBuffer/MTLBlitCommandEncoder/MTLBuffer objects.
gameCodeGuidance.currentInput: VirtualInput for keyboard-style state, VirtualPointerInput with PointerSample/PointerState for mouse, touch, and pen-style pointer state, VirtualGamepadInput with GamepadButton/GamepadAxis state for gamepad buttons and axes, mobile touch sample mapping into first-party PointerSample values without native handles, VirtualPointerInput::touch_gesture for active touch count, centroid, centroid delta, and two-touch pinch scale primitives, TouchGestureRecognizer for tap, double-tap, long-press, pan, swipe, pinch, rotate, and canceled gesture events, and MK_runtime RuntimeInputActionMap plus RuntimeInputStateView and RuntimeInputContextStack for deterministic GameEngine.RuntimeInputActions.v4 keyboard, pointer, gamepad button, scalar key-pair, and scalar gamepad-axis action documents evaluated over first-party virtual input state through bind_key, bind_pointer, bind_gamepad_button, bind_key_axis, bind_gamepad_axis, bind_key_in_context, bind_pointer_in_context, bind_gamepad_button_in_context, bind_key_axis_in_context, bind_gamepad_axis_in_context, context-aware action_down, and context-aware axis_value. Use RuntimeInputRebindingCaptureRequest, RuntimeInputRebindingCaptureResult, and capture_runtime_input_rebinding_action to turn the next allowed first-party key, pointer, or gamepad-button press into a validated RuntimeInputRebindingProfile action override candidate. Use RuntimeInputRebindingAxisCaptureRequest, RuntimeInputRebindingAxisCaptureResult, and capture_runtime_input_rebinding_axis to capture the first deterministic gamepad axis magnitude past a deadzone into a validated axis override candidate. Use RuntimeInputRebindingFocusCaptureRequest, RuntimeInputRebindingFocusCaptureResult, and capture_runtime_input_rebinding_action_with_focus when a host-owned menu has armed one capture row and needs deterministic focus_retained plus gameplay_input_consumed output for waiting, captured, or pressed-but-rejected candidates. Use RuntimeInputRebindingPresentationModel rows from make_runtime_input_rebinding_presentation when a runtime/game menu needs reviewed base/profile action and axis display labels plus symbolic glyph lookup keys. Empty context stacks use default; non-empty stacks select the first context that declares the requested action. Scalar action axes apply deterministic clamp/deadzone handling; broad UI focus integration, global input consumption/bubbling beyond focused rebinding capture, radial stick deadzones, response curves, pointer delta axes, gesture-to-RuntimeInputActions binding, full runtime/game rebinding panels outside the reviewed editor action capture lane, real platform input glyph generation/assets/rendering, keyboard-layout localization, keyboard key-pair axis capture, multiplayer device assignment, per-device profiles, SDL3, and native device handles remain follow-up adapter work.
gameCodeGuidance.currentInputContextPlanning: Use RuntimeInputContextStackRequest, RuntimeInputContextLayerDesc, RuntimeInputContextStackPlan, and plan_runtime_input_context_stack before evaluating RuntimeInputActionMap when generated games combine gameplay, menu, dialogue, rebinding, capture, or passive overlay layers. sample_2d_playable_foundation is the source-tree adoption reference for a passive HUD overlay plus gameplay stack. The planner validates context names, rejects duplicate contexts, supports default-context fallback, blocks lower-priority layers for modal rows, and reports gameplay_input_available, gameplay_input_consumed, ui_context_active, and capture_context_active without native handles, platform adapters, game-specific menu schemas, or a full UI focus/navigation system.
gameCodeGuidance.currentInputRebindingProfiles: MK_runtime RuntimeInputRebindingProfile and GameEngine.RuntimeInputRebindingProfile.v1 provide deterministic persisted full-row action and axis overlays over a caller-supplied base RuntimeInputActionMap. Use validate_runtime_input_rebinding_profile, apply_runtime_input_rebinding_profile, serialize_runtime_input_rebinding_profile, deserialize_runtime_input_rebinding_profile, load_runtime_input_rebinding_profile, and write_runtime_input_rebinding_profile through IFileSystem/RootedFileSystem. Use RuntimeInputRebindingCaptureRequest, RuntimeInputRebindingCaptureResult, and capture_runtime_input_rebinding_action for host-independent digital action trigger capture from first-party keyboard, pointer, and gamepad-button state into a validated action override candidate. Use RuntimeInputRebindingAxisCaptureRequest, RuntimeInputRebindingAxisCaptureResult, and capture_runtime_input_rebinding_axis for host-independent gamepad-axis and two-held-keyboard key-pair axis capture (capture_keyboard_key_pair_axes) from first-party VirtualGamepadInput axis magnitudes and VirtualInput key-down pairs into a validated axis override candidate. Use RuntimeInputRebindingFocusCaptureRequest, RuntimeInputRebindingFocusCaptureResult, and capture_runtime_input_rebinding_action_with_focus for an armed/focused/modal capture guard that reports focus_retained while waiting and gameplay_input_consumed for waiting, captured, or pressed-but-rejected digital candidates after the guard passes. Use RuntimeInputRebindingPresentationToken, RuntimeInputRebindingPresentationRow, RuntimeInputRebindingPresentationModel, present_runtime_input_action_trigger, present_runtime_input_axis_source, and make_runtime_input_rebinding_presentation for host-independent display rows with deterministic labels and symbolic glyph lookup keys after loading, validating, or applying a profile. Visible full interactive runtime/game rebinding panels outside the reviewed editor action capture lane, broad UI focus integration, global input consumption/bubbling beyond focused rebinding capture, radial stick deadzones, response curves, pointer delta axes, gesture-to-RuntimeInputActions binding, real platform input glyph generation/assets/rendering, keyboard-layout localization, keyboard key-pair axis capture, multiplayer device assignment, per-device profiles, removed SDL3 input APIs, editor-private runtime APIs, native device handles, and cloud/binary saves remain follow-up adapter work.

gameCodeGuidance.currentToolExecution: ShaderToolProcessRunner and ShaderToolExecutionPolicy bridge shader compile commands to platform process execution with artifact output-root policy and reviewed executable override for PATH names, project-relative tool paths, or absolute installed SDK/tool paths; ShaderArtifactValidationProcessRunner bridges spirv-val validation commands to the same shell-free process policy; DXC Vulkan SPIR-V command planning uses -spirv and -fspv-target-env=vulkan1.3 through executable-plus-argument vectors;
