discover_shader_tools finds deterministic dxc, spirv-val, metal, and metallib descriptors and tracks dxc.spirv-codegen capability markers for deterministic toolchain roots; evaluate_shader_toolchain_readiness reports D3D12 DXIL, Vulkan SPIR-V, DXC SPIR-V CodeGen, and Metal library readiness; editor ProjectShaderToolSettings executable values may be reviewed PATH tool names, project-relative tool paths, or absolute installed SDK/tool paths while working directory, artifact root, and cache index paths stay project-relative; make_spirv_shader_validation_command and execute_shader_artifact_validation_action provide reviewed SPIR-V artifact validation before native Vulkan shader module promotion; MK_editor_core ShaderToolDiscoveryState exposes discovered tools and readiness diagnostics to editor UI/AI workflows; pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/check-shader-toolchain.ps1 reports local shader tool blockers, reads Process/User/Machine environment state, searches MK_SHADER_TOOLCHAIN_ROOT, User/Machine PATH, Vulkan SDK variables, and vcpkg tool roots before Windows SDK fallback, runs a real quoted DXC SPIR-V CodeGen probe, and tools/check-shader-toolchain.ps1 -RequireAll can gate CI lanes; MK_editor_core ViewportShaderArtifactState verifies non-empty compiled D3D12 VS/PS DXIL bytecode and Vulkan VS/PS SPIR-V artifacts before native viewport promotion and can query readiness by shader id for material preview variants; build_shader_artifact_provenance records compiler, command, artifact, and input fingerprints for cache invalidation; ShaderArtifactCacheIndex and execute_shader_compile_action provide persistent shader cache index storage, cache-hit reuse, .geprovenance writes, deterministic test artifact marker writes, and real artifact enforcement for compiler-backed runs; build_shader_hot_reload_plan and build_shader_pipeline_recreation_plan turn stale provenance or successful non-cached compiles into explicit pipeline recreation decisions; build_shader_pipeline_cache_plan batches selected compile requests into deterministic hot-reload, compile-required, pipeline-recreation, cache-entry-current, and cache-index-repair rows; reconcile_shader_pipeline_cache_index repairs selected first-party cache indexes from current provenance without invoking compilers or writing artifacts; execute_asset_import_plan runs asset import or recook plans through IFileSystem, decodes first-party texture/mesh/audio/material source documents including optional hex byte payloads, optionally routes PNG, glTF, and common audio sources through MK_tools-owned asset-importers adapters when MK_ENABLE_ASSET_IMPORTERS is enabled, writes deterministic cooked artifacts preserving first-party byte payloads, decoded PNG RGBA8 payloads, decoded common-audio PCM payloads, and glTF triangle POSITION-only or POSITION/NORMAL/TEXCOORD_0/TANGENT interleaved mesh byte payloads for embedded/loaded buffers while rejecting partial normal/UV/tangent-frame primitives; execute_asset_runtime_recook builds recook plans, executes imports transactionally, stages successful cooked artifacts through AssetRuntimeReplacementState, and rolls every requested asset back on recook transaction failure; execute_asset_runtime_package_hot_reload_replacement_safe_point runs reviewed recook execution, passes helper-owned staged rows into commit_runtime_package_hot_reload_recook_replacement_v2, and commits only selected package matched AssetRuntimeReplacementState rows after resident replacement success; scan_asset_files_for_hot_reload turns registered asset files into deterministic hot reload snapshots; PollingFileWatcher turns IFileSystem roots into deterministic added, modified, and removed file events for AssetHotReloadRecookScheduler inputs; WindowsFileWatcher turns Win32 API ReadDirectoryChangesW notifications into FileWatchEvent records; LinuxFileWatcher turns inotify notifications into FileWatchEvent records; MacOSFileWatcher turns FSEvents notifications into FileWatchEvent records; native watcher handles stay backend-private and host-unavailable backends report explicit diagnostics
gameCodeGuidance.currentAssets: AssetRegistry, AssetDependencyGraph, AssetHotReloadTracker, AssetHotReloadRecookScheduler for debounce-based source and dependency-invalidated recook requests, AssetHotReloadApplyState for failed-reload rollback state, AssetRuntimeReplacementState for staging runtime asset replacements until a safe point, committing only selected staged assets, and rolling back failed recooks without losing the active asset, AssetCookedPackageIndex with content hashes, dependency edges, serialization, and deterministic recook decisions, deterministic GameEngine.Tilemap.v1 metadata through TilemapMetadataDocument with AssetKind::tilemap and tilemap_texture package edges while native GPU sprite batching, package-authored atlas metadata workflows beyond the reviewed decode/pack path, and production atlas editor UX remain follow-up, ShaderMetadataRegistry with descriptor reflection rows, shader compile command planning including DXC Vulkan SPIR-V commands with -spirv and -fspv-target-env=vulkan1.3, shader tool runner validation, compiler output capture contracts, shader include dependency discovery, shader include dependency graph edge generation, shader artifact manifest serialization including reflection rows, AssetImportMetadataRegistry, first-party texture/mesh/audio source document serialization with optional hex byte payloads, build_asset_import_plan, build_asset_recook_plan, MaterialDefinition, MaterialInstanceDefinition, deterministic material/material-instance serialization, material instance composition, fixed material factor uniform sizing, and explicit MaterialPipelineBindingMetadata for backend-neutral descriptor/pipeline layout derivation with uniform-buffer, sampled-texture, and sampler rows; optional PNG/glTF/common-audio source import adapters are implemented in MK_tools behind the asset-importers feature and feed this first-party cooked-artifact path; MK_tools preserves first-party texture data_hex, mesh vertex/index data_hex, and audio data_hex into deterministic cooked artifacts, decodes PNG RGBA8 payloads into texture data_hex, decodes common-audio PCM samples into audio data_hex, and cooks glTF triangle POSITION-only or POSITION/NORMAL/TEXCOORD_0/TANGENT interleaved accessors plus indexed/generated indices into deterministic mesh.vertex_data_hex and uint32 mesh.index_data_hex for embedded/loaded buffers; MK_runtime now reads cooked package indexes through IFileSystem, validates runtime payload hashes and dependencies, exposes first-party runtime asset handles, provides typed cooked texture/mesh/audio/material/scene/tilemap payload access including decoded optional byte payloads, serialized scene text, tilemap atlas texture path checks, and tilemap_texture edge inspection, resolves scene material dependencies through resolve_runtime_scene_materials, stages all-or-nothing package replacement at safe points, owns host-independent save/settings/localization/input action session documents, and exposes RuntimeDiagnosticReport helpers for package load, payload, scene, and session failures; MK_runtime_rhi creates backend-neutral RHI texture resources, mesh vertex/index buffer upload paths with derived position-only stride 12 or lit tangent-space stride 48 metadata, 256-byte-backed material factor uniform buffers with 64-byte factor payloads and one-pass Frame Graph command-list evidence, sampler-aware owner-device-validated material descriptor bindings, and submitted_fence rows from typed runtime payload uploads; MK_runtime_scene_rhi turns cooked package scene packets into retained SceneGpuBindingPalette resources through those typed runtime payload parsers and RHI upload helpers, preserves actual submit-order submitted_upload_fences rows, and reports submitted_upload_fence_count plus last_submitted_upload_fence rows; explicit-duration forward loop accumulation. MK_assets provides authored GameEngine.AnimationTransformBindingSource.v1 binding source rows through AnimationTransformBindingSourceDocument for mapping scalar curve targets to named transform components. MK_tools can derive those rows from glTF node TRS targets through import_gltf_node_transform_animation_binding_source using glTF node names or deterministic fallback names, and can import full unit-quaternion 3D node transform tracks through import_gltf_node_transform_animation_tracks_3d and bridge them into cooked quaternion clip source rows through import_gltf_node_transform_animation_quaternion_clip. MK_assets exposes GameEngine.AnimationQuaternionClipSource.v1 and GameEngine.CookedAnimationQuaternionClip.v1 for selected 3D TRS tracks, and MK_runtime exposes runtime_animation_quaternion_clip_payload for cooked package records. MK_runtime_scene resolves scalar binding rows for RuntimeSceneInstance data through resolve_runtime_scene_animation_transform_bindings, can apply caller-provided FloatAnimationCurveSample rows through apply_runtime_scene_animation_transform_samples, and can apply validated AnimationPose3d rows to named RuntimeSceneInstance nodes through apply_runtime_scene_animation_pose_3d. MK_scene_renderer can sample a cooked scalar AnimationFloatClipSourceDocument and apply AnimationTransformBindingSourceDocument rows to RuntimeSceneRenderInstance data through sample_and_apply_runtime_scene_render_animation_float_clip, then rebuild the render packet for generated 3D package smoke validation. MK_scene_renderer can also sample AnimationJointTrack3dDesc rows, apply named-joint AnimationPose3d rows to RuntimeSceneRenderInstance scene nodes, and rebuild the render packet through sample_and_apply_runtime_scene_render_animation_pose_3d. MK_scene_renderer can also sample cooked scalar morph-weight clips against RuntimeMorphMeshCpuPayload rows through sample_runtime_morph_mesh_cpu_animation_float_clip and return deterministic CPU-morphed positions for generated 3D package smoke validation. Generated DesktopRuntime3DPackage scaffolds and sample_desktop_runtime_game can load cooked animation_quaternion_clip records through runtime_animation_quaternion_clip_payload, decode them with make_animation_joint_tracks_3d_from_f32_bytes, and validate --require-quaternion-animation through sample_and_apply_runtime_scene_render_animation_pose_3d plus quaternion_animation_scene_applied/quaternion_animation_scene_rotation_z counters. JointLocalTransform remains the Z-only local pose contract for existing clips, while AnimationJointLocalTransform3d covers translation, positive scale, and unit-quaternion local rotations for 3D model-pose construction; AnimationJointTrack3dDesc can sample selected translation, quaternion rotation, and positive-scale tracks into AnimationPose3d with rest-pose fallback for missing components, and AnimationJointTrack3dByteSource plus make_animation_joint_tracks_3d_from_f32_bytes decode cooked little-endian component byte rows into those descriptors. MK_tools can import LINEAR glTF node TRS 3D rows through import_gltf_node_transform_animation_tracks_3d with full unit-quaternion rotation keyframes for caller-mapped AnimationPose3d workflows and can emit GameEngine.AnimationQuaternionClipSource.v1 rows through import_gltf_node_transform_animation_quaternion_clip. Backward root-motion accumulation, negative-time wrapping, inferred clip duration, tangent-space material/render integration beyond the cooked tangent-frame schema, broader GPU skinning parity, broader IK constraints beyond the implemented planar segment local Z and 3D swing/twist local clamps, authored IK assets, and animation graph authoring are not implemented yet.
gameCodeGuidance.currentAudio: AudioMixer with logical buses, voices, asset-id clip references, clip metadata registration, deterministic mix plans, spatial_mix_plan for listener/source distance attenuation and stereo pan metadata, AudioStreamingQueue for decoded chunk append/consume/starvation state, sample-frame render scheduling, streaming buffer availability, device format conversion intent, underrun diagnostics, AudioClipSampleData validation, selectable nearest/linear resampling quality, and render_interleaved_float output with gain, channel conversion, and clamped interleaved float PCM. AudioGameplayMixRequest, AudioGameplayMixPlan, and plan_gameplay_audio_mix provide the fail-closed game-facing cue/bus/trigger planner: game-owned music, sfx, ambience, ui, voice, and custom cue rows can describe clip ids, buses, gain, looping, pause/mute/fade, trigger start frames, gain scales, and optional spatial metadata before converting into mixer-ready AudioBusDesc and AudioVoiceDesc commands without native handles or codec assumptions. AudioDeviceStreamRequest, AudioDeviceStreamPlan, plan_audio_device_stream, and render_audio_device_stream_interleaved_float provide the device-independent stream pump baseline: host adapters report current queued frames and a target, MK_audio schedules bounded render chunks at device_frame + queued_frames, returns no_work when the queue is already full enough, and rejects invalid formats, invalid queue/render budgets, invalid resampling quality, and device-frame overflow. AudioProductionReviewRequest, AudioProductionReadinessPlan, and review_audio_production_readiness provide the selected production audio evidence gate for decoded source, streaming chunk, conversion, DSP graph, spatial/listener, HRTF host-gate, device lifecycle, unsupported claim, side-effect, diagnostic, selected-package, production-readiness, and replay-hash rows. Selected 2D/3D package samples can require --require-audio-gameplay-mixer and load cooked package audio payloads before reporting audio_gameplay_mixer_ready, bus/cue/trigger/command rows, paused/faded buses, looping/spatial commands, render frame/sample counters, sample absolute sums, and sample/payload diagnostics from first-party planner/mixer evidence without native device or codec breadth claims. sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package and sample_generated_desktop_runtime_3d_package emit audio_production_* selected package evidence with device and HRTF host gates still unavailable. Windows MK_audio_wasapi WasapiAudioRuntime/WasapiAudioDevice plans COM startup, opens the default eRender/eConsole shared-mode endpoint through private IAudioClient/IAudioRenderClient implementation, queues silence or rendered interleaved float PCM, reports queued frames, exposes pause/resume/clear controls, and records callback-free lifecycle evidence through wasapi_audio_device_lifecycle_evidence without public COM or WASAPI handles; platform/android contains a private AAudio output adapter that opens a low-latency float32 output stream, uses an atomic FIFO for callback-safe sample transfer, and starts/stops with Android lifecycle events without public AAudio handles; broad codec support, streaming decode integration, HRTF execution parity, broad DSP graph execution, device hotplug/selection parity, mixer authoring UI, middleware parity, and richer codec workflows remain follow-up work

gameCodeGuidance.currentNavigation: NavigationGrid, NavigationGridCoord, NavigationPathRequest, NavigationPathResult, NavigationPathStatus, and find_navigation_path provide a standard-library-only deterministic cardinal-grid A* foundation with walkability, positive traversal costs, stable tie-breaking, invalid/blocked endpoint diagnostics, no-path reporting, total path cost, and visited-node counts. NavigationPoint2, NavigationPointPathBuildResult, build_navigation_point_path_result, build_navigation_point_path, NavigationPathFollowRequest, advance_navigation_path_following, NavigationArriveSteeringRequest, and calculate_navigation_arrive_steering provide Path Following / Steering Foundation v0 for deterministic grid-path-to-point conversion, explicit target cursor advancement, max-step and arrival-radius movement, advanced/remaining distance reporting, and single-agent arrive steering desired velocity/speed. NavigationAgentConfig, NavigationAgentState, NavigationAgentUpdateRequest, NavigationAgentUpdateResult, make_navigation_agent_state, replace_navigation_agent_path, cancel_navigation_agent_move, and update_navigation_agent provide Navigation Agent Controller Foundation v0 for deterministic value-type agent movement state/ticks with idle/moving/reached/cancelled/invalid statuses, speed/delta clamping, final-destination slowing, path replacement, remaining-distance reporting, and desired velocity from actual path movement. NavigationGridPathValidationRequest, NavigationGridPathValidationResult, NavigationGridPathValidationStatus, validate_navigation_grid_path, NavigationGridReplanRequest, NavigationGridReplanResult, NavigationGridReplanStatus, and replan_navigation_grid_path provide Grid Dynamic Obstacle Replan v0 for validating remaining cardinal-grid paths against the current grid, reporting empty/start/goal/out-of-bounds/blocked/non-cardinal/unsupported-adjacency/cost-overflow diagnostics, reusing valid remaining paths, and recalculating deterministic replacement paths around newly blocked cells through find_navigation_path. NavigationLocalAvoidanceRequest, NavigationLocalAvoidanceResult, NavigationLocalAvoidanceDiagnostic, and calculate_navigation_local_avoidance provide Navigation Agent Local Avoidance v0 for finite-input single-agent desired velocity adjustment against explicit nearby neighbor rows, duplicate-neighbor and calculation-overflow diagnostics, deterministic neighbor ordering, closest-approach prediction over the requested time window, overlap/future-collision separation, applied-neighbor counts, and max-speed clamping. NavigationGridPathSmoothingRequest, NavigationGridPathSmoothingResult, NavigationGridPathSmoothingStatus, and smooth_navigation_grid_path provide Navigation Grid Path Smoothing v0 for reducing validated cardinal grid paths through deterministic walkable line-of-sight while preserving start and goal waypoints. NavigationGridAgentPathRequest, NavigationGridAgentPathPlan, NavigationGridAgentPathStatus, NavigationGridAgentPathDiagnostic, and plan_navigation_grid_agent_path provide Navigation Production Path v1 by composing pathfinding, optional smoothing, point-path mapping, diagnostics, point-count/cost evidence, and NavigationAgentState creation for generated-game route setup. NavigationNavmeshPathRequest, NavigationNavmeshPathResult, NavigationNavmeshDynamicObstacle, NavigationNavmeshReadinessConfig, NavigationNavmeshReadinessReport, plan_navigation_navmesh_path, and evaluate_navigation_navmesh_readiness provide Navigation Navmesh Production Path v1 by composing caller-authored scene-ref polygon rows, portal rows, deterministic dynamic-obstacle blocking, point-path mapping, status diagnostics, visited counts, cost evidence, and value-only readiness diagnostics for generated-game smoke proof. NavigationCrowdAgentDesc, NavigationCrowdPlanRequest, NavigationCrowdAgentPlanRow, NavigationCrowdPlanResult, NavigationCrowdPlanStatus, NavigationCrowdPlanDiagnostic, NavigationCrowdReadinessConfig, NavigationCrowdReadinessReport, NavigationCrowdReadinessStatus, NavigationCrowdReadinessDiagnostic, plan_navigation_navmesh_crowd, and evaluate_navigation_crowd_readiness provide Navigation Navmesh Crowd Readiness v1 for value-only deterministic crowd rows over navmesh routes, dynamic-obstacle participation, local-avoidance aggregation, max-agent budget rejection, duplicate/invalid-agent diagnostics, source-order checks, row budgets, readiness summaries, and stable replay counters. Generated games can use these for tile/grid movement, selected scene-ref polygon routing, value-only crowd movement authoring, and headless AI tests. navmesh asset import, spline/funnel smoothing, persistent/full crowd simulation beyond value-only batch planning, animation-aware steering, networked crowd determinism, RVO/ORCA, scene/physics integration, editor visualization, middleware, and background navigation jobs are not implemented yet.

AiPerceptionAgent2D, AiPerceptionTarget2D, AiPerceptionSnapshot2D, build_ai_perception_snapshot_2d, write_ai_perception_blackboard, AiPerceptionReadinessConfig, AiPerceptionReadinessReport, and evaluate_ai_perception_readiness_2d provide AI Perception Services v1 for explicit caller-supplied 2D sight/hearing classification, stable primary-target selection, duplicate target rejection, finite validation, blackboard projection into caller-selected fact keys, and readiness summaries over stable-primary, blackboard-projection, visible/audible-target, and target-budget evidence. The evaluator, authoring validator, and perception projection own validation, stable traversal/order, status propagation, visited-node traces, behavior-authoring trace rows, and missing-root, write_ai_perception_blackboard, evaluate_behavior_tree, plan_navigation_grid_agent_path, replan_navigation_grid_path, calculate_navigation_local_avoidance, plan_navigation_navmesh_path, gameCodeGuidance.currentJobExecutionWorkerPool: MK_core Job Execution Worker Pool v1 adds a real dependency-free first-party worker execution API for generated games, samples, and host adapters that need bounded CPU task execution before broader all-core optimization claims: JobExecutionPool, JobExecutionPoolDesc, JobExecutionBatchDesc, JobExecutionTaskDesc, JobExecutionContext, JobExecutionStopToken, JobExecutionRunResult, JobExecutionPoolStatus, JobExecutionRunStatus, JobExecutionDiagnosticCode, execute(batch), request_stop, stop_and_drain, worker_threads_started, job_execution_pool_status_label, job_execution_run_status_label, and job_execution_diagnostic_code_label. The pool is non-copyable and non-movable, starts explicit worker_count RAII-owned std::thread workers, owns bounded per-worker queues, supplies worker-local ScratchArena through JobExecutionContext, supports JobExecutionStopToken cooperative stop, drains each execute(batch) call before returning, catches task exceptions, and maps deterministic publish order plus queue/scratch/dependency evidence into JobSchedulingExecutionEvidence. It fail-closes invalid configuration, invalid tasks, queue overflow, blocked dependencies, dependency cycles, task exceptions, and stopped-pool execution. sample_desktop_runtime_game --require-job-execution-foundation reports package-visible job_execution_foundation_status=ready, job_execution_foundation_ready=1, job_execution_foundation_pool_status=ready, job_execution_foundation_run_status=ready, job_execution_foundation_worker_threads_started=2, job_execution_foundation_tasks_submitted=3, job_execution_foundation_tasks_executed=3, job_execution_foundation_tasks_failed=0, job_execution_foundation_task_side_effects=3, job_execution_foundation_execution_rows=3, job_execution_foundation_queue_rows=2, job_execution_foundation_deterministic_merges=3, positive job_execution_foundation_scratch_bytes, positive job_execution_foundation_scratch_high_water_bytes, and zero work-stealing/affinity/NUMA/SIMD/GPU-overlap/CUDA/HIP/SYCL side-effect counters. This is real worker-thread execution evidence only; it does not claim work stealing execution, affinity pinning, NUMA placement, hybrid P-core/E-core policy, SMT policy, SIMD dispatch, GPU async overlap, CUDA/HIP/SYCL paths, cross-vendor parity, cross-backend parity, or broad CPU/GPU/memory optimization readiness.
gameCodeGuidance.currentJobExecutionWorkStealing: MK_core Job Execution Work Stealing v1 adds opt-in work_stealing_enabled execution to JobExecutionPool and enable_work_stealing selection through JobExecutionTopologyPolicyDesc for generated games, samples, and host adapters that need first-party worker-queue stealing without OS affinity or vendor policy. JobExecutionRunResult reports work_stealing_applied, steal_attempt_count, steal_success_count, and worker_wait_count, and execute(batch) injects runtime steal/wait counters into JobSchedulingExecutionEvidence queue rows while preserving deterministic publish order and drain-before-return semantics. sample_desktop_runtime_game --require-job-execution-work-stealing reports package-visible job_execution_work_stealing_status=ready, job_execution_work_stealing_ready=1, job_execution_work_stealing_worker_threads_started=2, job_execution_work_stealing_tasks_submitted=2, job_execution_work_stealing_tasks_executed=2, job_execution_work_stealing_tasks_failed=0, job_execution_work_stealing_task_side_effects=2, job_execution_work_stealing_deterministic_merges=2, positive job_execution_work_stealing_steal_attempts, positive job_execution_work_stealing_steal_successes, positive job_execution_work_stealing_worker_waits, positive scratch byte/high-water counters, job_execution_work_stealing_applied=1, and zero affinity/NUMA/SIMD/GPU-overlap/CUDA/HIP/SYCL side-effect counters. This is narrow first-party work-stealing execution evidence only; it does not claim affinity pinning, NUMA placement execution, hybrid P-core/E-core policy, SMT policy, SIMD dispatch, GPU async overlap, CUDA/HIP/SYCL paths, cross-vendor parity, cross-backend parity, or broad all-core CPU/GPU/memory optimization readiness.
gameCodeGuidance.currentJobExecutionTopologyPolicy: MK_core Job Execution Topology Policy v1 adds portable worker-count selection for generated games, samples, and host adapters that need to size a bounded JobExecutionPool without claiming all-core or vendor-specific tuning: JobExecutionTopologyPolicyDesc, JobExecutionTopologyPolicy, JobExecutionTopologyPolicyStatus, JobExecutionTopologyPolicyDiagnosticCode, select_job_execution_topology_policy, observe_job_execution_logical_processor_count, job_execution_topology_policy_status_label, and job_execution_topology_policy_diagnostic_code_label. The policy treats observed logical processor count as explicit evidence, keeps a fallback count for hardware_concurrency=0 or missing evidence, applies requested worker count, worker count limit, and reserved logical processor rules, derives JobExecutionPoolDesc, emits JobWorkerTopologyRow, and reports host_evidence_required for missing processor-group or NUMA evidence. sample_desktop_runtime_game --require-job-execution-topology-policy reports package-visible job_execution_topology_policy_status=ready, job_execution_topology_policy_ready=1, job_execution_topology_policy_observed_logical_processors=8, job_execution_topology_policy_effective_logical_processors=8, job_execution_topology_policy_selected_worker_count=2, job_execution_topology_policy_worker_count_limit=2, job_execution_topology_policy_reserved_logical_processors=1, job_execution_topology_policy_fallback_used=0, job_execution_topology_policy_worker_count_limited_by_cap=1, job_execution_topology_policy_requested_worker_count_used=0, job_execution_topology_policy_clamped_to_logical_processors=0, job_execution_topology_policy_processor_group_count=1, job_execution_topology_policy_numa_node_count=1, and zero processor-group/NUMA/affinity/SIMD/GPU-overlap/CUDA/HIP/SYCL side-effect counters. This is portable worker-count policy evidence only; it does not claim work stealing execution, affinity pinning, NUMA placement execution, hybrid P-core/E-core policy, SMT policy, SIMD dispatch, GPU async overlap, CUDA/HIP/SYCL paths, cross-vendor parity, cross-backend parity, or broad CPU/GPU/memory optimization readiness.

Strict MK_scene composition remains index-based; the editor review model blocks composition until a reviewed mismatch row is resolved. resolve_prefab_variant_conflict removes redundant, later duplicate, or missing-node stale override rows, retargets a missing-node stale override when its hint uniquely resolves to one current base-prefab node without creating duplicate node/kind overrides, retargets an existing-node source_node_mismatch row when its hint uniquely resolves to a different current base-prefab node without creating duplicate node/kind overrides, or accepts the current indexed node for an existing-node source_node_mismatch by updating only source_node_name when no safe retarget exists. resolve_prefab_variant_conflicts applies all currently reviewed cleanup/retarget/accept-current rows by recomputing the review model after each row and returning a valid batch result. make_prefab_variant_conflict_resolution_action wraps valid cleanup/retarget/accept-current results as variant-scoped UndoStack snapshot actions for future visible MK_editor Apply controls, and make_prefab_variant_conflict_batch_resolution_action wraps a valid batch result as one variant-scoped UndoStack snapshot action for future visible MK_editor Apply All Reviewed controls. deserialize_prefab_variant_definition_for_review lets the editor load review-cleanup-repairable stale variants before saving only valid cleaned, retargeted, or accept-current-repaired variants. plan_prefab_variant_base_refresh, apply_prefab_variant_base_refresh, Continue first-party retained-mode MK_ui runtime UI, MK_ui_renderer, and MK_editor_ui editor model contracts from the implemented headless document/focus/style/text/image/layout/transition/binding/renderer-submission/text-image-accessibility-payload/monospace-text-layout/IRenderer-adapter plus UiRendererGlyphAtlasPalette glyph sprite baseline and UiRendererImagePalette colored sprite placeholder baseline, retained editor asset-pipeline, timeline, and recorder-backed profiler models into concrete renderer text/image/font/accessibility adapters, richer layout models, localization/accessibility metadata, and additional editor-shell adapters while keeping generated games on public mirakana::ui APIs instead of Dear ImGui, SDL3, UI middleware, or editor-private APIs.

Continue mobile from the implemented SDK-independent lifecycle/touch/orientation/safe-area/storage/permission contracts, Android GameActivity/NDK/Prefab template, Android Release Device Matrix v1 sample_headless Debug/Release/signing/API36-emulator evidence, Android native Vulkan surface creation bridge, Android AAudio output adapter, Android resource packaging/signing verification, deterministic Android AVD diagnostics, Android emulator smoke, iOS CMake/Xcode bundle template, iOS Metal-backed root view, iOS storage roots, iOS resource packaging/signing inputs, iOS Simulator smoke script, dedicated iOS GitHub Actions lane, blocker-aware pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/check-mobile-packaging.ps1 diagnostics, and Apple Metal iOS Host Evidence v1 apple-host-evidence-check host-gated diagnostics into actual Apple-hosted iOS simulator/device validation results, backend-neutral Apple Metal presentation, iOS signing validation, production Android signing/upload policy, physical-device coverage, broader ABI/device matrix coverage, and broader device/simulator CI lanes.
gameCodeGuidance.desktopRuntime3dSceneCollisionPackageSmo: DesktopRuntime3DPackage scaffolds and the committed sample can require --require-scene-collision-package on a selected installed package smoke that loads GameEngine.PhysicsCollisionScene3D.v1 as AssetKind::physics_collision_scene, preserves backend.native=unsupported, decodes RuntimePhysicsCollisionScene3DPayload rows with material and body.N.compound metadata, builds PhysicsWorld3D through build_physics_world_3d_from_runtime_collision_scene, and requires collision_package_status=ready, collision_package_ready=1, collision_package_diagnostics=0, collision_package_bodies=3, collision_package_triggers=1, positive contact/trigger-overlap evidence, collision_package_world_ready=1, collision_query_batch_ready=1, collision_query_batch_source_order_ready=1, collision_query_readiness_status=ready, collision_query_readiness_diagnostic=none, collision_query_readiness_diagnostics=0, deterministic collision query batch hit/no-hit/invalid-request counters, query-budget rejection counters, and gameplay_systems_collision_package_ready=1. This is a narrow first-party collision package smoke; exact AABB/sphere/vertical-capsule runtime queries now live in PhysicsWorld3D::exact_shape_sweep, deterministic collision query batches now live in PhysicsWorld3D::raycast_batch and PhysicsWorld3D::shape_sweep_batch, selected query readiness summaries now live in evaluate_physics_collision_query_readiness_3d, deterministic contact manifold rows now live in PhysicsWorld3D::contact_manifolds, opt-in CCD rows now live in PhysicsWorld3D::step_continuous, generated-game character/dynamic policy rows now live in evaluate_physics_character_dynamic_policy_3d, explicit distance-joint solving now lives in solve_physics_joints_3d, and opt-in distance/fixed/linear-axis positional constraint solving now lives in solve_physics_constraints_3d with default-unbounded max_rows row budgets and row_budget_exceeded diagnostics, value-only kinematic motion planning now lives in plan_physics_kinematic_motion_3d with deterministic slide/trigger/ground-probe rows, simple vehicle policy planning now lives in plan_physics_simple_vehicle_3d with deterministic wheel-probe rows, while host-independent replay signatures and count-based budget diagnostics now live in make_physics_replay_signature_3d and evaluate_physics_determinism_gate_3d, but dynamic-vs-dynamic TOI, rotational CCD, rotational rigid-body constraints, 2D CCD, persistent joint assets, broad vehicle dynamics/persistent vehicle simulation APIs, ragdolls, Jolt/native backends in the default/generated-game ready surface, broader middleware parity, and broad physics beyond the first-party Physics 1.0 ready surface remain separate follow-up work while `MK_physics_jolt` stays optional and dependency-gated.
gameCodeGuidance.currentRuntimeSimulationPersistence: Generated games can use mirakana::runtime::RuntimeSimulationPersistenceRequest, RuntimeSimulationPersistencePlan, RuntimeSimulationPersistentEntityRow, RuntimeSimulationPersistenceMigrationStep, RuntimeSimulationPersistenceDiagnostic, RuntimeSimulationPersistenceRemediationAction, and plan_runtime_simulation_persistence from MK_runtime after load_runtime_session_profile_documents when selected simulation resume needs stable save-slot, world id, snapshot id, world tick, entity state rows, supported schema migration chains, or corrupt-save remediation evidence. Non-save profile document failures do not block valid save recovery. The planner is value-only, reports RuntimeSimulationPersistenceDiagnosticCode rows for blocking save document status, corrupt save documents, missing save.slot/world.id/snapshot.id/world.tick evidence, malformed entity.<id>.(type|region|state_hash) rows, missing required entities, unsupported schema versions, invalid migration rows, and missing migration-chain steps, and leaves filesystem/package state untouched. RuntimeSimulationPersistenceRemediationAction is a recommendation only. This is not a cloud save system, cross-device sync, multiplayer replication layer, binary compatibility policy, platform user-directory resolver, migration executor, filesystem/package mutation helper, native handle surface, or game-specific component serializer beyond reviewed save keys.

steps: Run pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/agent-context.ps1.
Read engine/agent/manifest.json.
Create or update games/<game_name>/game.agent.json.

For AI Game Design Spec v1 generated 2D/3D package work, write or update fail-closed game.agent.json aiWorkflow.gameDesignSpec rows before implementation so gameplay family, template, camera, input map, core loop, scene list, asset requests, systems, package targets, validation recipe ids, quality gates, and unsupported claims reference only same-manifest package targets and validation recipes.

For AI Safe Content Mutation Ledger v1 work, keep game.agent.json aiWorkflow.contentMutationLedger rows fail-closed with reviewed mutation ownership, forbidden shared paths, and explicit package/CMake registration surfaces before writing generated game content.

For AI Placeholder Asset Pipeline v1 work, keep game.agent.json aiWorkflow.placeholderAssetPipeline rows limited to first-party placeholder assets, deterministic replacementWorkflow rows, package handoff counters, and no external asset downloads.

For AI Generated Game Playtest Loop v1 work, keep game.agent.json aiWorkflow.generatedGamePlaytestLoop rows tied to reviewed validation recipe surfaces, failure classification, and no validation weakening.

For AI Validation Remediation Recipes v1 work, keep game.agent.json aiWorkflow.validationRemediationRecipes rows scoped to common missing package file, invalid reference, host gate, shader/tool gap, counter mismatch, and runtime package load failures.

For ai-generated-game-quality-rubric-v1 work, keep game.agent.json aiWorkflow.generatedGameQualityRubric rows objective and evidence-based around objective, controls, feedback, deterministic-package-smoke, budget-evidence, unsupportedRows, and broad production readiness exclusions.

CI Matrix Contract Check v1 keeps ciMatrixCheck and classifyPrValidationTier aligned with tools/check-ci-matrix.ps1, tools/classify-pr-validation-tier.ps1, pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/check-ci-matrix.ps1, C++23 Release Package Artifact CI Evidence v1, Full Repository Static Analysis CI Contract v1, Assert-ReleasePackageArtifacts, Windows/Linux/sanitizer/macOS/iOS lane selection, static-analysis, and broader build/package CI execution evidence.
Use only public mirakana:: APIs from engine headers.

Use MK_core diagnostics/profile/trace export/review/reconstruction APIs for deterministic CPU observability; host adapters can attach a DiagnosticsRecorder and optional IProfileClock through DesktopHostServices to capture runtime_host.frame and renderer counters; use summarize_counter_budget and summarize_profile_budget for named nearest-rank count/min/average/p95/p99/max budget summaries with missing-sample diagnostics, invalid non-finite counter diagnostics, invalid-threshold diagnostics, and threshold status before optimization claims; use build_diagnostics_ops_plan plus export_diagnostics_trace_json, review_diagnostics_trace_json, and import_diagnostics_trace_json for dependency-free summary/trace/crash-review/telemetry handoff and first-party exported Trace Event JSON review/reconstruction instead of inventing game-local profiling formats, and do not expose native/backend handles.

Use MK_core ScratchArena, ScratchLease, ScratchLeaseStatus, and scratch_lease_status_label when a generated or sample game needs bounded frame temporary or per-worker scratch buffers through non-owning std::span<std::byte> leases from stable non-copyable/non-movable arena owners, explicit reset_at_safe_point boundaries, owner-checked worker releases, padding-aware capacity/high-water evidence, and memory_counter_row export. Use MK_core Memory Diagnostics v1 APIs when a generated or sample game needs memory evidence: MemoryCounterRow, MemoryLifetimeClass, MemoryDiagnosticsOptions, MemoryDiagnosticsSummary, MemoryBudgetPressure, MemoryDiagnosticsCode, MemoryDiagnosticsStatus, summarize_memory_diagnostics, and label helpers aggregate bytes/counts, reuse counts, reset counts, high-water bytes, budget pressure, stale-generation diagnostics, use-after-safe-point diagnostics, cross-thread-free diagnostics, and false-sharing diagnostics by lifetime class. sample_desktop_runtime_game --require-memory-diagnostics maps selected package resident CPU bytes, resident GPU bytes/budget, upload/staging bytes, transient GPU allocation counters, and framegraph aliasing-barrier evidence into package-visible memory_diagnostics_* rows including memory_diagnostics_status=ready, memory_diagnostics_ready=1, memory_diagnostics_diagnostics=0, positive memory_diagnostics_total_bytes, positive memory_diagnostics_package_resident_cpu_bytes, positive memory_diagnostics_resident_gpu_bytes, positive memory_diagnostics_upload_staging_bytes, memory_diagnostics_resident_gpu_pressure=nominal, positive memory_diagnostics_transient_gpu_aliasing_barriers, and memory_diagnostics_transient_gpu_framegraph_aliasing_ready=1 when multi-queue evidence is requested. Treat these rows as focused core scratch and diagnostics evidence only; they do not prove global allocator hooks/enforcement, broad pool policy, all-core CPU scheduling, NUMA/affinity, SIMD dispatch, CUDA/HIP/SYCL paths, automatic/LRU GPU residency, cross-vendor parity, cross-backend parity, or broad memory optimization.

Use MK_core Job Scheduling Evidence v1 Phase 3 APIs as host-independent scheduler evidence rows, deterministic bounded queue execution evidence, and selected package-visible scheduler evidence: JobWorkerTopologyRow, JobQueueCounterRow, JobSchedulingWorkItemRow, JobSchedulingExecutionOptions, JobSchedulingExecutionOrderRow, JobSchedulingExecutionEvidence, JobSchedulingDiagnosticsSummary, JobSchedulingDiagnosticsCode, JobSchedulingDiagnosticsStatus, summarize_job_scheduling_diagnostics, build_job_scheduling_execution_evidence, job_scheduling_diagnostics_code_label, and job_scheduling_diagnostics_status_label. Generated and sample games may report worker topology, explicit dependency ids, worker-local output counts, deterministic merge order, bounded queue capacity, queue depth high-water, submitted/completed jobs, steal attempts/successes, worker waits, blocked dependencies, dependency cycles, deterministic/nondeterministic merge counts, queue_rows, worker_scratch_rows, execution_order, per-worker MemoryCounterRow scratch charges, scratch bytes/high-water, scratch misuse, undersized job batches, and oversized job batches through these value rows. The selected sample_desktop_runtime_game --require-job-scheduling-evidence package smoke reports job_scheduling_evidence_status=ready, job_scheduling_evidence_ready=1, clean diagnostics, two workers, two queue rows, three submitted/completed/executed jobs, three deterministic merges, positive scratch byte/high-water counters, and zero native-thread/thread-pool/affinity/NUMA/SIMD/GPU-overlap side-effect flags. Generated games must still hand off requests for actual all-core CPU scheduling beyond JobExecutionPool v1, work stealing execution, affinity pinning, NUMA placement, hybrid P-core/E-core policy, SMT policy, SIMD dispatch, GPU async overlap, CUDA/HIP/SYCL paths, cross-vendor/backend parity, or broad CPU/GPU/memory optimization.

Use MK_core Job Execution Worker Pool v1 APIs when a generated game, sample, or host adapter needs real bounded CPU worker execution with deterministic evidence: JobExecutionPool, JobExecutionPoolDesc, JobExecutionBatchDesc, JobExecutionTaskDesc, JobExecutionContext, JobExecutionStopToken, JobExecutionRunResult, JobExecutionPoolStatus, JobExecutionRunStatus, JobExecutionDiagnosticCode, execute(batch), request_stop, stop_and_drain, worker_threads_started, and job_execution_* label helpers. The pool starts explicit RAII-owned std::thread workers, drains execute(batch) before returning, passes worker-local ScratchArena and JobExecutionStopToken into each task, reports actual worker_threads_started, catches exceptions, rejects invalid tasks and queue/dependency hazards, and maps deterministic publish evidence into JobSchedulingExecutionEvidence. The selected sample_desktop_runtime_game --require-job-execution-foundation package smoke reports job_execution_foundation_status=ready, job_execution_foundation_ready=1, two worker threads, three submitted/executed task bodies, three deterministic merges, positive worker scratch bytes/high-water, and zero work-stealing/affinity/NUMA/SIMD/GPU-overlap/CUDA/HIP/SYCL side-effect counters. Generated games may use Job Execution Work Stealing v1 for opt-in work stealing, but must still hand off requests for affinity pinning, NUMA placement, hybrid P-core/E-core policy, SMT policy, SIMD dispatch, GPU async overlap, CUDA/HIP/SYCL paths, cross-vendor/backend parity, or broad CPU/GPU/memory optimization.

Use MK_core Job Execution Work Stealing v1 APIs when a generated game, sample, or host adapter needs opt-in first-party work stealing over a bounded JobExecutionPool: set JobExecutionPoolDesc.work_stealing_enabled directly or set JobExecutionTopologyPolicyDesc.enable_work_stealing before using the derived JobExecutionPoolDesc. JobExecutionRunResult reports work_stealing_applied, steal_attempt_count, steal_success_count, and worker_wait_count, and execute(batch) maps runtime steal/wait counters into JobSchedulingExecutionEvidence queue rows without changing deterministic publish order. The selected sample_desktop_runtime_game --require-job-execution-work-stealing package smoke reports job_execution_work_stealing_status=ready, job_execution_work_stealing_ready=1, two worker threads, two submitted/executed task bodies, two deterministic merges, positive steal attempt/success/wait counters, positive worker scratch bytes/high-water, job_execution_work_stealing_applied=1, and zero affinity/NUMA/SIMD/GPU-overlap/CUDA/HIP/SYCL side-effect counters. Generated games must still hand off requests for affinity pinning, NUMA placement execution, hybrid P-core/E-core policy, SMT policy, SIMD dispatch, GPU async overlap, CUDA/HIP/SYCL paths, cross-vendor/backend parity, or broad CPU/GPU/memory optimization.

Use MK_core Job Execution Placement Policy v1 APIs when a generated game, sample, or host adapter needs host-independent CPU placement evidence before OS affinity or vendor policy execution: JobExecutionPlacementPolicyMode, JobExecutionPlacementPolicyDesc, JobExecutionPlacementPolicy, select_job_execution_placement_policy, placement status/diagnostic label helpers, inherited JobExecutionPoolDesc, NUMA/core-type/SMT evidence fields, and explicit side-effect flags. The selected sample_desktop_runtime_game --require-job-execution-placement-policy package smoke reports job_execution_placement_policy_status=ready, job_execution_placement_policy_ready=1, job_execution_placement_policy_requested_mode=os_default, job_execution_placement_policy_selected_mode=os_default, job_execution_placement_policy_host_evidence_required_diagnostics=1 for a missing NUMA probe, inherited two-worker work-stealing evidence, NUMA node count 1, zero performance/efficiency core counts, SMT evidence unknown, and zero affinity/NUMA/SIMD/GPU-overlap/CUDA/HIP/SYCL execution counters. Generated games may use this as evidence for policy intent, but must hand off requests for Windows CPU Set execution, Linux affinity execution, NUMA allocation execution, hybrid P-core/E-core pinning, SMT scheduling, SIMD dispatch, GPU async overlap, CUDA/HIP/SYCL paths, cross-vendor/backend parity, or broad CPU/GPU/memory optimization.

Use Windows CPU Set Worker Placement v1 only through host-owned Windows lanes when a generated game or sample needs package-visible Windows CPU Sets worker-start placement evidence: MK_core exposes JobExecutionWorkerPlacementRequest, JobExecutionWorkerPlacementResult, JobExecutionWorkerPlacementCallback, placement mode fields on JobExecutionPoolDesc, and worker placement attempt/applied/diagnostic/selected-CPU-Set run counters, while MK_platform_win32 owns Win32CpuSetRow, Win32CpuSetWorkerPlacementPlan, query_win32_cpu_sets, select_win32_cpu_set_worker_placement, and make_win32_cpu_set_worker_placement_callback. The selected sample_desktop_runtime_game --require-windows-cpu-set-worker-placement package smoke reports windows_cpu_set_worker_placement_status=ready, windows_cpu_set_worker_placement_ready=1, positive windows_cpu_set_worker_placement_selected_cpu_sets, two worker rows, two worker placement attempts/applies, selected_cpu_set_applications=2, native_thread_handles_exposed=0, and zero Linux affinity / NUMA allocation / hybrid-SMT / SIMD / GPU async / CUDA / HIP / SYCL side-effect counters. Windows CPU Set SMT Worker Placement v1 is the focused Windows-only follow-up: sample_desktop_runtime_game --require-windows-cpu-set-smt-worker-placement selects avoid_smt_siblings, orders distinct CoreIndex rows before SMT sibling CPU Sets, reports windows_cpu_set_smt_worker_placement_status=ready, selected_mode=avoid_smt_siblings, positive distinct core and SMT sibling CPU Set counters, smt_topology_known=1, smt_policy_applied=1, native_thread_handles_exposed=0, and zero hybrid P-core/E-core / Linux affinity / NUMA allocation / SIMD / GPU async / CUDA / HIP / SYCL side-effect counters. Generated games must still hand off requests for Linux affinity, NUMA memory locality, hybrid P-core/E-core pinning, broad SMT scheduling quality, SIMD dispatch, GPU async overlap, CUDA/HIP/SYCL paths, cross-vendor/backend parity, or broad CPU/GPU/memory optimization.

Use MK_core Job Execution Topology Policy v1 APIs when a generated game, sample, or host adapter needs portable worker-count selection before constructing a bounded worker pool: JobExecutionTopologyPolicyDesc, JobExecutionTopologyPolicy, select_job_execution_topology_policy, observe_job_execution_logical_processor_count, derived JobExecutionPoolDesc, fallback/cap/reserve rules, processor-group evidence flags, and NUMA evidence flags. The selected sample_desktop_runtime_game --require-job-execution-topology-policy package smoke reports job_execution_topology_policy_status=ready, job_execution_topology_policy_ready=1, observed/effective logical processors = 8, selected worker count = 2, worker count limit = 2, reserved logical processors = 1, cap-limited selection = 1, and zero processor-group/NUMA/affinity/SIMD/GPU-overlap/CUDA/HIP/SYCL side-effect counters. Generated games may request enable_work_stealing for the separate work-stealing path, but must still hand off requests for broad all-core scheduling, affinity pinning, NUMA placement execution, hybrid P-core/E-core policy, SMT policy, SIMD dispatch, GPU async overlap, CUDA/HIP/SYCL paths, cross-vendor/backend parity, or broad CPU/GPU/memory optimization.
Do not use Dear ImGui, SDL3, editor, UI middleware, or editor-private APIs for game runtime HUDs or menus; target first-party mirakana::ui contracts.

When a generated or sample game needs a reusable unsupported engine capability, stop at AI Engine Capability Handoff v1: use mirakana::EngineCapabilityHandoffRequestRow plus mirakana::review_engine_capability_handoff_request and optional game.agent.json aiWorkflow.engineCapabilityHandoffs descriptor rows to record the canonical capability id, blocked feature, current workaround, affected game files, desired first-party public contract, and required evidence without editing engine internals, exposing native handles, or leaking SDL3/Dear ImGui/renderer/RHI/backend/middleware contracts.

When authored source assets and Scene v2 data need a host-independent runtime scene package, use the validated authored-to-runtime workflow register-source-asset -> cook-registered-source-assets -> migrate-scene-v2-runtime-package -> validate-runtime-scene-package. Select validate-runtime-scene-package inputs from game.agent.json runtimeSceneValidationTargets rows after the package exists, and stop there unless a selected renderer or desktop recipe adds its own host-gated validation.

Register headless/default games with MK_add_game in games/CMakeLists.txt; use pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/new-game.ps1 -Name <game_name> -Template DesktopRuntimePackage for config-only desktop package scaffolds, pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/new-game.ps1 -Name <game_name> -Template DesktopRuntimeCookedScenePackage for first-party cooked-scene desktop package scaffolds, pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/new-game.ps1 -Name <game_name> -Template DesktopRuntime2DPackage for generated host-gated packaged 2D gameplay with cooked sprite/material/audio/orthographic scene payloads, pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/new-game.ps1 -Name <game_name> -Template DesktopRuntime3DPackage for generated host-gated packaged 3D gameplay with cooked texture/mesh/morph_mesh_cpu/material/animation_float_clip/animation_quaternion_clip/perspective scene/physics-collision payloads plus transform/morph/quaternion scene-transform package smoke, selected safe-point package streaming smoke, selected renderer quality package smoke, selected scene collision package smoke, and primary camera/controller package smoke, pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/new-game.ps1 -Name <game_name> -Template DesktopRuntimeMaterialShaderPackage only when a scaffold needs source material/HLSL authoring inputs plus host-built desktop shader artifacts, or MK_add_desktop_runtime_game with GAME_MANIFEST plus mirakana::Win32DesktopGameHost only when selecting the optional desktop runtime lane, and declare config/assets/cooked package payloads in runtimePackageFiles with PACKAGE_FILES_FROM_MANIFEST so they ship beside the executable. Cooked-scene, packaged-2D, and packaged-3D manifests must also declare runtimeSceneValidationTargets rows with safe game-relative .geindex paths and explicit scene asset keys for validate-runtime-scene-package, plus host-gated-safe-point packageStreamingResidencyTargets rows that reference those validation targets and .geindex package files for selected safe-point package streaming execution. DesktopRuntime2DPackage manifests that opt into reviewed sandbox authoring must declare sandboxWorldAuthoringTargets rows for tile definitions, palette brushes, chunk templates, procedural seeds, changed files, tilemap package changed files, and dependency edges; source authoring review documents stay out of runtimePackageFiles while --require-sandbox-authoring-review validates package-visible counters and zero external image decoding, downloads, importer plugin execution, native handles, renderer/RHI residency, runtime source parsing, and package apply during review. Source-tree desktop runtime builds stage registered package files and shader artifacts beside a target-specific executable directory to avoid cross-target generated payload collisions, and generated 2D/material-shader/3D package targets get DXC-available source-tree shader-artifact CTests before installed package validation when they declare selected D3D12 shader artifacts. Source material and HLSL files remain authoring inputs and must not be listed in runtimePackageFiles, runtimeSceneValidationTargets, or packageStreamingResidencyTargets. Use the editor Apply Package Registration button or tools/register-runtime-package-files.ps1 -GameManifest games/<game_name>/game.agent.json -RuntimePackageFile <game-relative-file> after reviewing package draft rows, then run schema/package validation. Use literal CMake PACKAGE_FILES only when intentionally mirroring the manifest.

Run pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/prepare-worktree.ps1 after entering a manual linked worktree so ignored worktree roots, external/vcpkg, and any existing local vcpkg_installed package tree are ready before configure. Register auto-merge with gh pr merge --auto --merge --match-head-commit <headRefOid> and leave branch deletion to cleanup because gh pr merge --delete-branch also deletes the local branch. After a task-owned PR is merged into main, run pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/post-merge-task-cleanup.ps1 -WorktreePath <path> [-HeadRefOid <headRefOid>] from Local or another non-target checkout; GitHub delete_branch_on_merge removes merged head branches and the wrapper always passes -DeleteLocalBranch -DeleteRemoteBranch to tools/remove-merged-worktree.ps1. See docs/workflows.md step 9 for reachability checks and cleanup guards. Run pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/check-toolchain.ps1 when diagnosing local CMake, CTest, CPack, clang-format, Visual Studio, MSBuild, or PATH issues; toolchain-check also enforces that visible CMake configure/build presets inherit normalized-configure-environment / normalized-build-environment so raw cmake --preset commands inherit PATH and unset Path, repository wrappers collapse parent PATH/Path variants into one child Path on Windows and PATH elsewhere for CMake/MSBuild/CTest, and reports linked-worktree vcpkg readiness; treat generated out/build/<preset> trees as disposable outputs and do not repair them by editing CMakeCache.txt, .vcxproj, .sln, or MSBuild props; use pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/cmake.ps1 and tools/ctest.ps1 for focused local CMake/CTest loops; tools/build.ps1 and tools/test.ps1 default to automatic CMake/CTest parallelism through official --parallel options and accept -Jobs <N> only when a local host needs an explicit lower throttle; MSVC targets use bounded /MP2 plus /Zf through MK_apply_common_target_options; time-sensitive host-resource tests use CTest RUN_SERIAL rather than disabling suite-wide parallelism; reserve raw cmake --preset checks plus check-toolchain.ps1 -RequireDirectCMake for explicit direct-command requirements; use pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/check-format.ps1 / pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/format.ps1 for repository formatting because raw clang-format --dry-run commands require direct-clang-format-status=ready or an explicit resolved tool path, and those wrappers also check/apply tracked text normalization (LF, UTF-8 without BOM, a single final newline, and no trailing EOF blank lines); use tools/check-text-format.ps1 / tools/format-text.ps1 for text-only loops; run pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/validate.ps1 before completion; validate.ps1 runs independent static checks through bounded parallel jobs with per-check timeouts before the sequential build, clang-tidy smoke, and CTest gate, writes complete per-script logs under ignored out/validation-logs/ while keeping console output bounded, accepts -SkipTidySmoke only when another selected lane owns full static-analysis evidence, accepts -StaticJobs <N> only when a local host needs an explicit static-check throttle, and accepts -StaticCheckTimeoutSeconds <N> only for host-specific timeout diagnostics. Hosted workflow checkouts stay SHA-pinned with permissions: contents: read and persist-credentials: false, then retry once after transient fetch/auth failure before toolchain steps; ccache stats are guarded so early checkout/toolchain failures keep the primary diagnostic visible. Hosted CI uses validate.ps1 -StaticOnly -StaticJobs 1 -StaticCheckTimeoutSeconds 120 for the dedicated static lane and validate.ps1 -SkipStaticChecks -SkipTidySmoke for the Windows MSVC lane to avoid duplicate static validation; raw Linux, sanitizer, and macOS lanes pass explicit official --parallel job counts to CMake build and CTest. Windows vcpkg package/install caches and build caches restore with actions/cache/restore and attempt fresh non-blocking actions/cache/save writes only after validation succeeds. Run pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/validate-desktop-game-runtime.ps1 when the game selects desktop-game-runtime, run pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/package-desktop-runtime.ps1 for the default sample-shell desktop-runtime-release lane, run tools/package-desktop-runtime.ps1 -GameTarget <target> for a registered desktop runtime package target with package smoke metadata and package file validation, run the explicit real-window D3D12 or Vulkan smoke command only on hosts with the matching runtime/toolchain support, and use pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/build-editor.ps1 for the optional native Win32 first-party retained desktop editor shell lane; MK_editor_core remains covered by default validation.
